programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A woman from Cabe's past asks for help in apprehending a Serbian war criminal, so the team travels to Cuba to track him down. Walter faces a judge following his car accident

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Confidential files are leaked by an anonymous hacker, who threatens to release more unless the Navy agrees to renew a contract with a struggling shipbuilding firm. The Navy Secretary suspects the company's owner Philip Wickes is to blame, and asks NCIS to join forces with the PsyOps unit in an elaborate ruse to bring the culprit out of hiding. Meanwhile, Gibbs' colleagues begin to notice his attraction to his opposite number Samantha Ryan. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Vance's brother-in-law Michael Thomas is linked to the murder of a petty officer, and Gibbs is placed under pressure to clear the man's name. Meanwhile, a negative performance review leaves Tony feeling downhearted and he resolves to prove the assessment wrong by being less chatty while on duty. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine lieutenant's body falls from the sky and the team links the man's death to the disappearance of a Navy chaplain thought to have been kidnapped by rebels working with Colombian crime cartels. Ziva and Tony are dispatched to conduct a rescue operation and enlist the help of former Interpol agent Monique Lisson, while Gibbs discovers there was more to the chaplain's outreach mission than meets the eye. Crime drama, guest starring Karina Lombard (The L Word) and Jamie Lee Curtis (A Fish Called Wanda)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A fire crew discovers a man's body after putting out a raging blaze at a warehouse belonging to a Navy-contracted electronics company, and Gibbs recognises the victim as Carter Plimpton - a former member of an NCIS black-ops unit. Meanwhile, a member of the Baltimore police department helping with the case appears to have a grudge against Tony

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's mission to track the arsonist targeting US Navy facilities appears to end in success when the man in question is killed by a thermite explosion on board the USS Brewer. Meanwhile, Abby studies evidence relating to fire-starter Bruce Johnson, who died when his car exploded, and discovers coded messages in his quarters that indicate he may have been working as part of a larger gang. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Return of the military drama. The team is reunited when a nuclear-powered satellite is knocked out of orbit. Meanwhile, Paige and Walter confront their feelings

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A woman from Cabe's past asks for help in apprehending a Serbian war criminal, so the team travels to Cuba to track him down. Walter faces a judge following his car accident

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team attend the funeral of a fallen colleague before resuming their hunt for the man they believe to be the port-to-port killer. Vance frantically researches the suspect's background to learn more about his motivations and discover who his next target could be - but one of the investigators follows a clue that leads to danger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony tries to piece together the events that led to him lying wounded in a hospital bed, having been shot in the chest through his bulletproof vest. Under the guidance of therapist Dr Rachel Cranston, his memory begins to return and suspicion falls on his ex-lover EJ Barrett. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Sarah Jane Morris

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators examine the death of US marine Tommy Hill, who appears to have been fatally stabbed en route to a homecoming party thrown in his honour by his foster family. McGee and Ziva follow a trail of blood that leads them to a mobile phone, while Abby discovers the victim had been trying to hack into Virginia's state website and Ducky's autopsy reveals that Hill had been injected with a sedative

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A man who died after being knocked down by a van is revealed to be a professional hitman responsible for at least six contract killings - and his next target was to be a navy lieutenant commander. Abby is delighted at being a match for a teenager who needs a kidney transplant, but she is informed that someone else with very similar DNA has also been identified as suitable. Has she found a relative she never knew about?

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter, Cabe and Happy get trapped aboard a top-secret submarine stuck at the bottom of the ocean, and battle to make it out alive before the self-destruct function activates

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is murdered at the home of Gunnery Sergeant Holt Perkins, and while the victim is initially assumed to be the marine's wife, analysis reveals her to be a convicted thief, presumed to be on the scene as part of a plot to hit Perkins' house. When the agents discover the calling card of notorious terror cell Dragon's Tooth on the body, it becomes clear the murder has been committed as part of a wider agenda, and the team joins forces with the Department of Defence to catch the killer. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A prostitute and what seems to be one of her clients are gunned down in a drive-by shooting, and when it emerges that the would-be John was actually a Navy officer, Gibbs and DiNozzo realise the killings are connected Lex Tarlionas, an incomplete operation to bring justice to those responsible for an attack on a US airport in 1979, which left 100 people dead. The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US marine visits the home of his former captain, only to find the man has been killed, and the killer is waiting inside the property. While the soldier escapes with his life, the attacker gets away too. It quickly emerges the prime suspect is Qasim, an Afghan translator evidence, and evidence points against him, until it is discovered he has a brother, who is loyal to the Taliban. The subsequent chain of evidence leads Gibbs and Bishop to Afghanistan

programme.desc: Sylvester goes undercover in a federal prison on a mission to save the lives of three judges, but when his life is threatened, the team has to break him out

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A Navy officer dies following a confrontation involving a dancer and a Sudanese emigre at a strip club. However, when investigations reveal he was a code-breaker involved in naval intelligence, Callen and Sam dig deeper and gradually unearth links to Safar, a Libyan-born terrorist known to recruit on behalf of al-Qaeda. Henri Lubatti guest stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A taxi driver recently released from prison is murdered just hours after sending an e-mail to an old alias that Callen used when he went undercover to investigate three marines who stole $5million. Resuming the persona, Callen goes in search of the gang, one of whom is killed in a drive-by shooting

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam and the team spring into action when fellow agent Dom activates a distress signal. They track down his car, which is riddled with bullet holes, and Eric intercepts a signal from his phone, tracing it to a house that contains nothing but some used medical supplies and a dead body. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

Daniela Denby-Ashe, Lesley Manville and Sinead Cusack star

programme.title: North and South

programme.desc: Hi-Ho tackles the toughest challenge of his career, while Elaine faces a terrifying ordeal which forces Adam to confront his family, and there is a shock in store for Pearce

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew does his best to pour oil on troubled waters in Liverpool, while back at the Beeches, new locum Nick Goodson arrives full of high hopes. Elsewhere in Cardale, a popular local man goes on trial accused of child abuse - and Erica's role as chief prosecution witness makes her the target of a hate campaign. Saskia Wickham, Gary Mavers and Thomas Lockyer star

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paige treats a pub fight witness suffering from bizarre mental flashbacks, all of which involve visions of a corpse. It isn't long before the body in question turns up, presenting Cramer with a fresh mystery to solve. Nigel Havers and Jane Gurnett star

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey has a surprising announcement to make, while Sharon's pregnancy quest carries on unabated and Dorien at last finds a way to make the most out of Marcus. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Diana breaks Tom out of hospital, Harvey decides getting married will help him secure membership of the golf club, and Geoffrey believes his wife is evil

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: German generals plan to meet at the cafe disguised as onion sellers, but so does a new group of shot-down British airmen. Second World War comedy set in German-occupied France, starring Gorden Kaye

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A friend's lack of interest in life prompts Compo, never one to shy away from danger, to volunteer to test Wesley's heavy-duty sucker and blower, which can only mean disaster. Roy Clarke's comedy, with Bill Owen, Gordon Wharmby and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives return home after their trip to South Africa, to find Nonnatus House has come under new management. The formidable Sister Ursula (Harriet Walter) has taken charge and introduced some drastic changes to the way the clinic is run, which Sister Julienne, bound by her vow of obedience, is powerless to oppose. The midwives come to the aid of a victim of domestic violence whose husband is about to be released from prison, and who risks losing everything if she seeks a divorce. Sister Mary Cynthia suffers further anxiety attacks, and Patsy receives distressing news about her father

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Ursula insists the midwives do not become emotionally involved with their patients, but her harsh new approach proves hard to cope with in the face of two devastating cases. A father-to-be is badly injured in an explosion, leaving him unable to work to support his family, and a couple with dwarfism expecting their first child are warned that their child may be stillborn, a situation that leaves Patsy confronted with one of the hardest decisions of her life

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reopens the investigation into an actress's disappearance when clips of her being assaulted in a 1990 movie are posted on the internet. Director Don Maddox features in the footage, but when questioned, he appears to have problems distinguishing fact from fiction. A potential witness is then found dead, and Brian and Jack are seconded to the unit handling the case, only to find Emily Driscoll (Dennis Waterman's daughter Hannah) is heading the operation. Imogen Stubbs and Sandra Dickinson also guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Double bill. A rising star in the Essex police force is found beaten and shot in his burnt-out car in what proves to be an emotional case for his colleagues. The team helps DCI Jim Sullivan, the detective charged with bringing the killer to justice, and it isn't long before suspicion falls on Dean Fallon - a local drug dealer and the son of a known crime boss. But when he proves to have a solid alibi, the investigation shifts closer to home. Starring Emilia Fox and David Caves, with James Alexandrou and Jason Watkins

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry poses as a hitman in a plan to entrap a gambling shack owner determined to dispose of his wife's lover. The operation takes on extra urgency when it emerges the runaway couple have booked one-way tickets to Australia for the next day. Roger is furious with Lewis when the pair manage to lose a prisoner after the ambulance they are travelling in is involved in an accident. Emma's complicated love-life reaches crisis point when her ex finds her in the pub

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this episode, the contestants include a pest controller, a slam poet and a train signaller

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob is furious to discover that Connie has cancelled the nurses' training in favour of the doctors, and at a board meeting, the tension between the pair continues to build. Overwhelmed with work and an ever-increasing workload, Noel makes a terrible mistake which leads to the death of a patient. Meanwhile, Loz stands up to Rory once and for all, freeing the team from Rory's control, and opinions in the ED are divided when an illegal stowaway falls from the sky. Starring Amanda Mealing and Charles Venn

programme.title: Gravity (2013)
programme.desc: Two astronauts are left stranded in orbit above the Earth when the debris from an exploding satellite destroys their shuttle. Left drifting in space with no way to contact mission control, they begin a perilous journey to reach the safety of a space station before their oxygen supply runs out. Thriller, starring Sandra Bullock and George Clooney

programme.title: Jason Manford's Muddle Class
programme.desc: The stand-up performer and actor presents a wealth of new material about growing up 'working class' then finding, over the years, that part of him has become 'middle class'

programme.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron (2015)
programme.desc: The superhero team faces a threat from within when Iron Man creates an artificial intelligence to take their place protecting the world. The android grows resentful of his creators, and decides his mission is best accomplished by wiping out the human race. Meanwhile, two vengeful superpowered twins seek to turn the Avengers against each other. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Hemsworth, Chris Evans and Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Ben Thompson and Sally Nugent

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Angellica Bell take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Paul McDonald needs to convince his wife Christine their future lies in Australia, so they head to Brisbane for a week to check out properties, work opportunities and the lifestyle

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the transformation of a terraced house in Abbey Wood, south-east London, as well as a seven-year project on a large period house in Torquay

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at an antiques fair in Stamford, Lincolnshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp challenges the conkers world champion

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits the Salvation Army's Hadleigh Farm in Essex to celebrate more than 150 years of this Christian denomination, still going strong in the 21st century

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah goes property hunting in rural Devon with a couple who have a £350,000 budget and hope to find a home that has both a workshop and an art studio

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Bauhaus-Galan. Gabby Logan introduces live coverage from the fourth Diamond League event of the season, which is held at Stockholm Olympic Stadium. Britain's Holly Bradshaw will be competing in the women's pole vault, up against Greece's Katerina Stefandi, who edged her out of the medals at the last World Championships. The men's event features young Swedish star - and another Monaco winner - Mondo Duplantis. With analysis by Colin Jackson

programme.title: Attenborough and the Sea Dragon
programme.desc: The naturalist joins a team of scientists examining the 200 million-year-old fossilised remains of an ichthyosaur, a vast sea creature which was discovered on Dorset's Jurassic Coast. As the skeleton is reassembled, advanced imaging technology and computer animation is used to create a simulation of the sea creature as it would have appeared when it was still alive - the most detailed reconstruction of its kind ever attempted. Meanwhile, forensic investigators painstakingly examine the bones to solve the mystery of how the ichthyosaur died

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on the Somerleyton Estate in Suffolk to find out about an ambitious project aiming to turn East Anglia into a giant nature reserve. Adam Henson fears the worst for his winter barley as he gets harvesting

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from underneath the last Concorde to ever fly at Aerospace in Bristol, which is celebrating 50 years since its maiden flight. Objects brought in to be valued include a Fijian war club, a tiny Chinese vase, a collection of Radiohead memorabilia, a four leaf clover brooch, a rare piece of Venetian glass and a set of silver candlesticks

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: The general election is approaching and while Kabir remains desperate to make amends, Lata finds herself courted by Amit and Haresh in Calcutta. Maan is once again separated from Saeeda when he accompanies his father on the campaign trail to the Nawab's village seat in Rudhia. Andrew Davies' adaptation of Vikram Seth's novel, starring Tanya Maniktala

programme.title: The Notebook (2004)
programme.desc: An elderly man reads a love story to a woman in a nursing home, from an old notebook full of sentimental scribblings about a passionate young couple whose fledgling affair is smothered almost before it has begun. Romantic drama, starring Ryan Gosling, Rachel McAdams, Gena Rowlands and James Garner

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Sally Nugent

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Yana the tiger cracks a canine tooth and it must be removed, but it is not a simple procedure and the anaesthetic does not go to plan. Kate Humble and Ben Fogle present

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore a two-bed terrace in Salford, a three-bed semi in Derbyshire and a one-bedroom flat in south London

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value to take to auction. Away from the contest, Charlie also meets a familiar face with a passion for Georgian glass

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine suffers an indignity at a gig, and Shannon reacts badly to rejection

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: TV doctors David Bull, Ellie Cannon, Linda Papadopoulos, Ranj Singh and Javid Abdelmoneim take on the general-knowledge champions to win a cash prize for charity. Jeremy Vine asks the questions

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is in Cornwall, on hand to help a couple from London make their first joint property purchase with a budget of £750,000. Margherita also makes time to help out the head gardener at Trebah, one of the Great Gardens of Cornwall

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: A window cleaner, a children's party entertainer and a train supervisor compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each spending a day working at the other's jobs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell go in search for items in Cumbria, setting out from Cockermouth and heading towards an auction near Kendal

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball presents the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe has been held prisoner in Iran for more than four years after being convicted of spying charges. Nobody knows how many other British citizens have been imprisoned by the Iranian authorities, because the UK government refuses to say. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre meets the families of some of those who have been arbitrarily detained and asks whether the payment of a historic debt could set them free.

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Linda Henry and Luisa Bradshaw-White, aka sisters Shirley and Tina Carter, who talk about their characters' most memorable moments and revealing some of their best behind-the-scenes stories. Linda chats about how fans react to seeing 'Shirley' in the street, Luisa recalls her own brush with musical superstardom, and the pair explain how a tender scene quickly became a riot of laughter and chaos every time the cameras cut

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Learning that their posh neighbours the Beg-Chetwyndes have an ancient claim over their land, Mike and Alison throw an upmarket dinner party to help resolve a thorny issue. But with the ghosts her only guide to high-class decorum, the evening starts badly and only gets worse - thanks to Mary's woeful catering, Mike's clueless deal-making and Robin's raucous celebration of the lunar eclipse. Elsewhere, the spooks become obsessed with a TV sitcom. Comedy, guest starring Geoff McGivern and Sophie Thompson

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Maan's disgrace has a disastrous effect on the Kapoor family, but when the bad press is used against his father in the general election campaign, it results in an unexpected gesture from an old friend. Following the revelation of further interference from her meddling family and emboldened by casting her vote in India's first national democratic election, Lata makes a decision about her future. Concluding part of Andrew Davies' adaptation of Vikram Seth's novel, starring Tanya Maniktala

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: The comedy sketch show returns, pulling back the curtain on interracial adverts and discovering what happens when someone reveals spoilers for a TV series. The E19 posse do their bit for the environment, there is an episode of Jamaican Countdown, and the African aunties learn their nephew's secret. Starring Vivienne Acheampong and Samson Kayo

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: The Handmaid's Tale star Amanda Brugel is the guest host as the contestants are paired up with newcomers from LGBTQI charity Rainbow Railroad for a makeover challenge

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor David Tennant takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from April 2019, with Conservative MP Johnny Mercer and comedian Zoe Lyons joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Unsuspecting parents are shipwrecked by daughter Elvie as their wedding day takes a very wet and windswept turn. However, being shipwrecked on a desert island is only the start of the wedding day surprises. After all, it can't be a real desert island without a treasure hunt can it?

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood investigate odd auditory occurrences, including a man who collects worms by grunting, and a gang of prairie dogs doing a Mexican wave

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends compete in the World Gurning Championships in Egremont

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield build an 'adventure den' for nine-year-old Rufus, who has leukaemia, and loves climbing around even though he cannot always join in with his friends

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall challenges a cheetah to a race and meets a cute but deadly bird

programme.title: Rise of the Continents
programme.desc: Geologist Professor Iain Stewart examines key moments in the development of the world's major land masses. He begins by looking at how Africa was formed from the wreckage of a long-lost supercontinent, with its landmarks, mineral wealth and wildlife providing clues that help piece together the story of its creation. He also reveals how this deep history has left its mark on modern day Africa and the world

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A millionaire tries to save jungle tigers on the Indonesian island of Sumatra by capturing them in their natural habitat, then transporting them to his own land and setting them free. However, zoologist Alan Rabinowitz is unsure about the wisdom of his scheme, particularly given the dangers the big cats pose to humans, and decides to investigate further

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary following life on farms in Scotland and the Lake District. In Armadale, a hill farmer prepares for one of Europe's biggest sheep sales, while in Haweswater, a couple take on the biggest gamble of their lives by moving to a larger farm 10 miles north. An Aberdeenshire couple who began farming four years ago plan to raise baby ostriches, and a Northumberland shepherd prepares for the National English Sheepdog trials, and new farmers hatch ostriches

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson reveals a secret passion when she prepares her dark and deviant liquorice pudding, then extols the virtues of fregola - the Sardinian sun-dried and toasted couscous - with tomato and clams. Her dinner-party guests are served butterflied leg of lamb and baby aubergines with oregano and roasted red onion, followed by meringue gelato cake with sharp raspberries and dark chocolate sauce for dessert

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers create classic puddings and explore the history and traditions behind Britain's favourite desserts, demonstrating recipes for a creamy chicken, ham and leek pie and the perfect apple pie

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: An enthusiastic Leona McCalla takes her husband and daughters Down Under, to see if they can share the love of Australia that she has developed on previous trips. Leona's hope is that they will agree to make the current temporary stay permanent. Nicki Chapman follows the clan's experiences

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner accompanies Johnny Ball on a trip to his childhood home of Bristol, where he recalls his love of maths challenges in the classroom and the impact of ration books on the supply of sweets at his local corner shop

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: The former Strictly Come Dancing judge takes ex-Homes Under the Hammer presenter Lucy Alexander back to Herne Bay in Kent to reminisce about a holiday she had in 1979

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores nature's evolutionary anomalies, beginning by examining how the unique features of the chameleon and the giraffe have benefited them in their respective environments. He then looks at curious creatures that have led to accusations of forgery, such as the duck-billed platypus and the midwife toad

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Professor Doug Emlen examines how animals use their bodies as weapons in epic battles to gore, stab, crush and batter their adversaries to death, from elephants to rhinoceros beetles, assessing whether they are more than just instruments of blunt force. For the professor this has been a lifetime's ambition to discover the secrets of nature's arms races, what triggers them and what they can teach us about the most formidable weapons on Earth - our own

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt search for items in Kent to take off to auction. Catherine falls for a colourful brooch and Jonathan unearths a horn beaker used by Winston Churchill. Host Paul Martin uncovers the story of Dover Castle's secret tunnels.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: This time, Michael Portillo ventures on a rail journey through Germany, guided by Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide published in 1936. Starting in the vibrant city of Berlin, Michael visits the Museum of Modern Art in Berlin's Kreuzberg, where he sees how a leading artist of the era, George Grosz, warned of the rise of fascism in a haunting self-portrait. In the Schöneberg district of the capital, Michael researches the decadent night scene of the 1920s, and looks at how cabaret culture is being revived today. When he reaches Weimar, Michael investigates the beginnings of Bauhaus design and visits the movement's first building, before travelling onto Stuttgart, where he takes a ride in a high-performance Porsche to the manufacturer's Stuttgart headquarters

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Highlights of the second day of the Third Test, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton. The hosts were victorious in a remarkable match at the start of the series, defying the odds on the final day to chase down their target, but they'll be hoping for a more consistent performance in this match against the youthful tourists

programme.title: Sean Connery: In His Own Words
programme.desc: Friends and actors pay tribute to Sean Connery, in a programme first shown in 2015 as the movie legend reached his 85th birthday. Dougray Scott, Robert Carlyle, Pierce Brosnan, Terry Gilliam and George Lucas are some of the big names who discuss Connery's iconic Bond movies, as well as many of his other films, including The Untouchables, for which he won the Best Supporting Actor Oscar

programme.title: Entrapment (1999)
programme.desc: An elusive professional art thief suspected of stealing a priceless painting is pursued by an insurance agent. However, instead of bringing the criminal to book, she teams up with him to plot the robbery of a lifetime - although neither is sure the other can be trusted. Crime thriller, starring Sean Connery, Catherine Zeta-Jones, Ving Rhames, Will Patton and Maury Chaykin

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A red-hot selection of summer sounds, featuring songs by John Travolta and Olivia Newton-John, the Undertones, Shaggy, Bananarama, Bobby Goldsboro, Bay City Rollers, Fun Boy Three, the Style Council and Don Henley. Other archive sizzlers include DJ Jazzy Jeff and the Fresh Prince, the Sundays, Sabrina, Chris Rea, the Barracudas, Zoe, Martha and the Muffins, Bryan Adams, Girls Aloud and ELO

programme.title: Golf: Women's British Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights from the third day at Royal Troon in South Ayrshire. Hinako Shibuno of Japan moved into the lead at this stage last year in Milton Keynes and went on to finish one stroke clear of America's Lizette Salas to claim the title

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode embarks on an alphabetical journey through dishes prepared by an array of TV chefs

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani are in Northumberland in the coastal village and ancient English royal kingdom of Bamburgh. Matt discovers what it takes to safeguard the castle from the elements, while Anita finds out about the secrets of bones dating back to the seventh century. Tom Heap investigates the new trend to escape the city for the country, and Adam Henson goes head to head with his actor neighbour Robert Llewellyn to discover how green machines size up to traditional diesel

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don makes plans for next spring by planting some early flowering wildflowers. He also gives his recipes for the different compost mixes he employs to take successful cuttings. Nick Bailey continues his quest for the South African origins of much-loved summer bulbs, while in the heart of London the programme meets a keen gardener who has filled her houseboat with ornamental and edible plants. Plus, a trip to Preston to visit a woman passionate about propagation and, in particular, pineapples

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter looks over her annual wildflower mixes in her rural Aberdeenshire garden and George Anderson reviews his bedding plant containers in Joppa. There is also a film from 21-year-old Calum Clunie, the youngest allotment holder in Leven, whose plot is bursting at the seams with colour and produce

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: A Cook Abroad
programme.desc: The MasterChef judge fulfills a lifelong ambition to go to Argentina, where he embarks on an epic road trip to find the best cut of steak in the world and the ideal method of cooking it. Along the way, John discovers that modern industrial farming has changed the landscape of the pampas and threatened a way of life unchanged for centuries. He joins a group of gauchos on a working livestock ranch, before ending at a restaurant in Mendoza where cooking beef has become an art form

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Falklands War veteran and charity campaigner Simon Weston takes Brian Turner back to his childhood home of Nelson, in Caerphilly, where the chef prepares two dishes from his youth

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: The Strictly Come Dancing judge takes model, actress and cook Lisa Faulkner back to West Wittering in West Sussex to relive the favourite childhood holidays she took in 1980

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste discuss the personal and social benefits of spreading happiness to others, from video games in church to spending a day as a clown

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Weather forecaster Carol Kirkwood takes a journey through the world of horticulture, receiving inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters. The series progresses in alphabetical order, and this programme features themes beginning with the letter A, and includes archive material from Gardeners' World, Countryfile and The Gardener's Year

programme.title: Le Mans (1971)
programme.desc: A racing driver battles against the odds to recover from a serious injury, determined to take on his arch rival in the prestigious 24-hour competition - and win a widow's heart. Drama, starring Steve McQueen, Elga Andersen, Ronald Leigh-Hunt and Christopher Waite

programme.title: Murray Walker: A Life in the Fast Lane
programme.desc: A profile of the motor-racing commentator whose voice became one of the most recognisable on British television. The programme follows Murray as he travels to the 2011 Australian Grand Prix, looks back at highlights from his career on radio and TV and recalls his time in the Army. Featuring contributions by Nigel Mansell, Damon Hill, Michael Schumacher, Jenson Button and Lewis Hamilton

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: More highlights from the last two series of the motoring show, including the team taking a summer road trip in £500 convertibles, Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff taking up the brutal motorsport of Formula Off Road, and the Dallara Stradale being put through its paces on the Top Gear track

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Highlights of the third day of the Third Test, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton. This is the final match of the series and the second consecutive one to be staged at this ground, with the hosts in particular becoming familiar with the venue, having also played here recently against the West Indies

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer return to Scotland for their latest fishing trip. Having failed to catch a salmon in the River Tay, they hope to have more luck on the Tweed. They begin their journey at the isolated Bemersyde Estate before moving on to the Bridge Pool at Kelso, one of the most famous salmon fishing spots in Scotland. They also try their hand at boat fishing and receive some mystical predictions from a familiar face

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian offers his unique take on different TV genres, beginning with a guide to the history of soap operas. Harry touches on everything from The Grove Family and The Newcomers to EastEnders and Coronation Street, while also giving viewers a few helpful tips on how to make their own domestic drama

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Panellists Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies and Theo Paphitis assess the profit-making potential of an inventive new solution to clearing up post-festival rubbish, a means of balancing gut bacteria from a sauerkraut enthusiast, and a new kind of phone alarm app. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Last week saw the return of the comedy show in which three comedians take the stage of the Hammersmith Apollo in west London. The premise is simple. One comic acts as host, warming up the audience before introducing short sets by two other stand-ups. Future editions promise the likes of Ed Byrne, Rob Beckett, Joel Dommett, Henning Wehn, Lucy Porter, but tonight Nish Kumar plays host, before handing over to David O'Doherty and Luisa Omielan

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Hiro conquers his doubts over his heroic powers, while Claire uncovers the truth behind a missing video tape. Niki confides in a friend when her son becomes embroiled in a crisis and Peter assists Nathan and his wife in their campaign to win the election

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Mohinder is plagued by strange visions of a mysterious boy when mourning his father in India. Hiro and Ando meet an interesting person during their trek to New York, while Matt and Audrey follow a promising lead in their search for the elusive Sylar, and are surprised by where it takes them. Masi Oka and Sendhil Ramamurthy star

programme.title: Golf: Women's British Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights from the fourth and final day at Royal Troon in South Ayrshire, where Hinako Shibuno of Japan was the reigning champion

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: A look at the consequences of the 2003 invasion of Iraq by the US-led coalition, with America forced to return to confront the brutal legacy when Isis emerges to take over huge swathes of the country. Omar Mohmamed, a university professor from Mosul, recalls how the night armed men invaded his city, proclaiming they were the Islamic State. For the next three years, he lived under one of the most brutal regimes the world has ever known, witnessing daily executions, hand cutting and stonings

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: The return of the programme following the hunt for missing people, from the police trying to finding them to loved ones left at home. The first edition focuses on Cheshire police's search for Mark Smith, an ex-soldier with PTSD who went missing after posting on social media that he wanted to end it all. Sgt Paul Chadwick assesses the case and is immediately concerned that a trained soldier in such a volatile state could pose a high risk - especially as Mark has previously stated that he would like to provoke police to shoot him. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Climbing guide Jack, master tea-taster Sebastian and luxury couture retailer Onita go head to head in a bid to prove their job is the hardest and claim a £1,000 prize. Day one is steep challenge for Onita as she finds out how many times she's expected to climb one of London's most iconic tourist attractions. The next day sees Jack pushed for time as he grapples with the complexity of grading and blending tea leaves. On the final day, Jack and Sebastian try to master the science of window dressing and selling high-end women's clothing in a luxury boutique

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste discuss the personal and social benefits of spreading happiness to others, from video games in church to spending a day as a clown

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Mozart's situation improves after she is let back into the gang, but the Whiskers suffer a tragedy when they battle with the Commandos

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's 2,500-mile rail tour of south-east Asia reaches Malaysia. On the island of Penang, he traces the origins of the former British colony in Georgetown, rides one of the world's steepest funicular railways and is enchanted by exotic specimens at Malaysia's first butterfly sanctuary. Travelling on the nation's hi-tech railway network Michael visits its wettest town, Taiping, and learns how the discovery of tin made this land a prized possession for Britain. Next stop is the regal town of Kuala Kangsar, home to the Sultan of Perak and to one of Malaysia's most prestigious schools

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Evans family from Kent sample life in Brisbane, with mother-of-four Sue hoping to convince her husband and eldest daughter that the move is just what they need

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Miranda Krestovnikoff and Simon King join team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke on the panel show that tests wildlife knowledge, with Kate Humble asking the questions

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George tells Sarah she must be clever about how to approach the woman she suspects of being a former concentration camp guard, while Douglas realises that his condition is terminal

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter V, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Incredible Medicine: Dr Weston's Casebook
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston continues to unlock the secrets of the human body, hearing the stories of a man who fixed his own heart and a girl whose immune system attacked her own brain. Dr Weston also hears the remarkable story of a man who can taste words, and how his condition is helping develop new ways for blind people to navigate and recognise colours

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates this documentary following a couple of mountain lion families in Wyoming's Rocky Mountains. The dramatic story of two mothers struggling to raise their cubs is giving scientists an invaluable insight into the behaviour of these elusive predators

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Anita Manning value items brought to Blackburn Cathedral, while Paul Martin learns about how mill workers in the town used to be woken by a human alarm clock

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angellica Bell, Gyles Brandreth, Scarlett Moffatt and Iain Stirling test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: The horticultural designer encourages people across Ireland to get out in their gardens, taking calls, answering questions and offering a range of practical tips. He helps design a garden for Mary Fitzpatrick in Co Wicklow and creates a sunken fire pit and seating area in his own patch

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Highlights of the fourth day of the Third Test, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton, as the teams looked to conclude the series on a high

programme.title: Tom Kerridge: Lose Weight for Good
programme.desc: The chef recruits and mentors a group of struggling dieters, offering them advice on ways they can lose weight with long-term results. After losing over 12 stone himself through a combination of diet and exercise, Tom uses his own personal experiences to help the 13 dieters reduce their calorie intake, demonstrating how they can create their own simple, low-calorie dishes. Meeting the group for the first time, Tom lays out what they can expect from their weight-loss journey, and begins with tackling the issue of comfort eating, a problem that many of the group struggle with

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The seventh first-round match sees four students from the University of Bristol pit their wits against a team from Corpus Christi College, Oxford in a bid to reach the second stage of the quiz. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Unbelievable Story of Carl Beech
programme.desc: Vanessa Engle's documentary telling the story of the man who claimed he had been sexually abused by a group of prominent men in the 1970s and '80s, naming Jimmy Savile, ex-prime minister Edward Heath and former home secretary Leon Brittan as among those involved. The film is constructed using Beech's own words, taken from media and police interviews and from his blogs, and there are interviews with those who were close to the story at the time, including his ex-wife and Diana Clemetson, the widow of Lord Brittan

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis look back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Angela Barnes, Gary Delaney, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman and Nish Kumar. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Vienna, the city that once ran the Austro-Hungarian Empire and continues to be home to comfort dishes such as tafelspitz and goulash

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Michael Mosley investigates recent scientific research that could change the way people exercise, including a study that suggests many could benefit from just three minutes of high-intensity activity a week. He also discovers the health benefits of seemingly innocuous actions, such as walking and fidgeting, and learns why some people do not respond to exercise at all

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani are in Northumberland in the coastal village and ancient English royal kingdom of Bamburgh. Matt discovers what it takes to safeguard the castle from the elements, while Anita finds out about the secrets of bones dating back to the seventh century. Tom Heap investigates the new trend to escape the city for the country, and Adam Henson goes head to head with his actor neighbour Robert Llewellyn to discover how green machines size up to traditional diesel

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A metal sculptor, a stained-glass designer and a textile artist pitch ideas for a Derby couple's wedding, with only two being made before the buyers choose their favourite

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: After advertising her home on Airbnb, Mandy takes in a guest from Russia - and they soon become more than friends. But can he be trusted? Comedy, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with Tony Way (After Life)

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Lola has not had a customer in her beauty salon for two days so Mandy suggests she introduce fish pedicures. Only to buy the wrong kind of fish. Sean Lock guest stars in the comedy, with Diane Morgan and Michelle Greenidge

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Central Scotland, Bobby and Anne must gather their pregnant ewes from the hills ready for lambing, while east of Inverness, Mel and Martin move into a caravan on site to deal with the imminent arrival of hundreds of lambs. On the Isle of Lewis, Sandy makes tweed curtains for the holiday let as Easter approaches, and an unexpected tragedy hits the Lennoxes

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin's epic expedition from the North Pole to the South one. In the first leg of his journey, he leaves the Arctic and travel by a variety of vehicles through Russia, Norway and Finland. He interviews Father Christmas, takes a sauna with a Finnish MP and nearly starts a new life in a Norwegian lighthouse along the way

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The murderer launches a twisted plan to highlight cutbacks in mental health care - by dispatching schizophrenic people to commit synchronised violent crimes in Malmo and Copenhagen. Saga and Martin hope a teenage runaway could prove to be the witness who gives them a vital break in the case. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Several police officers are found innocent of beating an immigrant to death, but one of them is kidnapped. He is later found by the victim's brother - who then faces an agonising choice over whether to forgive him or exact revenge. Meanwhile, Saga makes contact with the killer for the first time. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The murderer holds a bus full of schoolchildren hostage, and vows he will only let his captives go if members of the public set fire to the premises of five companies that indirectly benefit from child labour. Meanwhile, Saga becomes convinced the culprit is one of her fellow police officers. Crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: A witness statement leads Saga and Martin to arrest one of their colleagues on suspicion of masterminding the crime spree, but he appears to have an alibi for all the incidents. Meanwhile, the murderer announces he has achieved his goals, and declares his reign of terror has come to an end - though Saga is certain his campaign is far from over. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: Avicii: True Stories (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the Swedish DJ, who died in 2018, featuring extensive personal and family archive and behind-the-scenes footage to paint a picture of his life

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Documentary examining Peter Sutcliffe's crimes and asking whether 1970s attitudes to women influenced the investigation, leaving the murderer free to kill again. The first episode explores the early years of the investigation from 1975, when the first Ripper murder occurred. The location of the first two murders in Chapeltown, then well-known as Leeds' main red light district, led the police to decide that prostitution was the connection between the attacks. After the second murder in January 1976, the police announced they were hunting a 'prostitute killer' and the investigation became driven by this theory

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: A journey around some of the most dramatic coastlines in Europe, uncovering the stories behind how these landscapes have shaped history

programme.title: BBC Proms Classics
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined by conductor Gustavo Dudamel to present his 2007 debut Prom leading the Simon Bolivar National Youth Orchestra of Venezuela. The performance included Shostakovich's Tenth Symphony, Bernstein's Symphonic Dances from West Side Story, and a feast of musical gems from Latin American composers Márquez, Ginastera and Gutiérrez

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: Profile of the novelist and poet Vikram Seth, who has also won awards for his travel writing, including footage from a family reunion in Delhi

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Internationally-renowned Malian photographer Malick Sidibé talks about what inspired his work and the history and culture of his homeland, from the American-style rock and roll days of the 1950s and 1960s to the austere Islamic conservatism imposed since the military coup in 1968

programme.title: On Camera: Photographers at the BBC
programme.desc: The working practices, lives and opinions of some of the greatest photographers of the last 60 years, drawn from the BBC's archives. The programme brings into focus the key genres - fashion, portraiture, documentary and landscape - and the characters behind the camera who have helped defined them, including Norman Parkinson, David Bailey, Eve Arnold and Martin Parr

programme.title: Britain in Focus: A Photographic History
programme.desc: Eamonn McCabe explores the development of the art of photography in the UK, beginning by looking at how science and technology allowed pioneering photographers like Roger Fenton and Julia Margaret Cameron to create a new art form in the 1800s

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea's habit leads her into danger, Tiffany decides to solve a problem by helping herself to someone else's plant pot, and the Millers can't believe their good fortune

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea takes advantage of Patrick's good intentions, the Millers insist Keith makes up for his carelessness and Bradley is nervous about his lunch date with Maggie

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick resorts to desperate measures to save Chelsea from a path of self-destruction, Jase has a surprise lined up for Dawn, and Ricky and Bianca try to get Liam to take a test

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Dawn is suspicious of Jase's behaviour, but is shocked by what she discovers, Chelsea reaches rock bottom and Bradley grows concerned his reputation may be on the line

programme.title: Handmade in Africa
programme.desc: Seydou Kane crafts a new kora from scratch in his studio in downtown Dakar, Senegal, an instrument also known as a West African lute or harp that dates back to the 13th century

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that includes luxurious green foliage and an old, weathered covered bridge placed lovingly among all the beauty

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: Ade explores central Africa, travelling through Gabon, the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. He investigates plans to develop a sustainable version of palm oil farming, meets a champion boxer helping to rehabilitate child soldiers, and talks to one of Africa's most outspoken political campaigners. He also reports on how conflict between militia groups influenced by foreign powers lies at the heart of the problems that have made the Democratic Republic of Congo arguably the most chaotic and corrupt country in Africa, as well as discovering how the conflict has impacted on the country's few remaining gorillas

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Afua Hirsch explores the art, music and culture of Senegal, discovering a country with a cultural influence far beyond its size, with dynamic film, fashion and hip-hop scenes

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates Jr focuses on an era of great commercial and manufacturing growth throughout several regions on the continent. Beginning with the revolutionary transformation of North and West Africa, he travels to the shores of the Sahara Desert, where farmers, traders, warriors and nomads turned this region into the crossroads of some of history's most advanced, and wealthiest, civilizations

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates Jr looks at the powerful, cosmopolitan cities that dotted Africa at the time when Europe was in its Middle Ages. From 1000 to 1600, commerce, wealth and prosperity expanded across Africa, building new cities and founding new powerful states that mark this golden age

programme.title: Inside Cinema: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Part one of two. A look at family in cinema in all their maddening, dysfunctional glory, with clans in Aliens, Back to the Future, Home Alone, Tokyo Story, Pan's Labyrinth and Gosford Park among those analysed. Kathy Burke narrates

programme.desc: Issie gets a new vision of Mystic running through the forest along an old railway track, and wonders if this might be the clue that will lead her to Blaze's herd. After some encouragement, Issie decides to ride out on Blaze to find the tracks, and Caroline, Caleb, Dan and Natasha join her on the mission. As they journey deeper into the forest, past their favourite swimming hole, the kids make a series of bewildering discoveries and eventually realise they're completely and utterly lost. With one of the gang's future ambitions at stake, they face a race against time to get back to the stables. The fisherman wakes to discover a long, long, long fish at the end of his line. The Twirlywoo Screen draws a small line that Chickedy and Chick help make longer. Toodloo helps the line get a little longer still but when Great BigHoo taps the screen, the line becomes so long it shoots off the Twirlywoo Screen. programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jason Atherton and Andi Oliver and special guest Jessie Ware via the internet. Plus, clips from the BBC food archive, featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Nigella Lawson

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: The theme this week is 'eat local' as Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond cook goat curry with chapatis. They are joined by farmer Jillian McEwan and actor and comedian Sanjeev Kohli, who selects a Scottish cider to accompany the food

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits properties in Preston and Liverpool in need of renovation, and Dion Dublin is in Nottingham to meet a couple with differing views on their project

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at the International Antiques Fair of Wales in Builth Wells, Powys. Charlie Ross visits the National Fencing Musuem

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Catalans Dragons v Wakefield Trinity (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the sixth-round tie at John Smith's Stadium in Huddersfield. The Dragons won this competition for the first time in their history in 2018 and are expected to be in contention once again, while Trinity are looking to end a 57-year drought in the Challenge Cup. With commentary by Matt Newsum, and analysis by Jon Wilkin and Jamie Peacock

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a writers special edition of the general knowledge quiz. Contestants are Laurence Marks, Maurice Gran, Terry Deary, Eve Pollard, Jojo Moyes, Jeffery Deaver, Peter James and Dreda Say Mitchell

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this episode, the contestants include a pest controller, a slam poet and a train signaller

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob is furious to discover that Connie has cancelled the nurses' training in favour of the doctors, and at a board meeting, the tension between the pair continues to build. Overwhelmed with work and an ever-increasing workload, Noel makes a terrible mistake which leads to the death of a patient. Meanwhile, Loz stands up to Rory once and for all, freeing the team from Rory's control, and opinions in the ED are divided when an illegal stowaway falls from the sky. Starring Amanda Mealing and Charles Venn

programme.title: Gravity (2013)
programme.desc: Two astronauts are left stranded in orbit above the Earth when the debris from an exploding satellite destroys their shuttle. Left drifting in space with no way to contact mission control, they begin a perilous journey to reach the safety of a space station before their oxygen supply runs out. Thriller, starring Sandra Bullock and George Clooney

programme.title: Jason Manford's Muddle Class
programme.desc: The stand-up performer and actor presents a wealth of new material about growing up 'working class' then finding, over the years, that part of him has become 'middle class'

programme.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron (2015)
programme.desc: The superhero team faces a threat from within when Iron Man creates an artificial intelligence to take their place protecting the world. The android grows resentful of his creators, and decides his mission is best accomplished by wiping out the human race. Meanwhile, two vengeful superpowered twins seek to turn the Avengers against each other. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Hemsworth, Chris Evans and Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Ben Thompson and Sally Nugent

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Angellica Bell take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Paul McDonald needs to convince his wife Christine their future lies in Australia, so they head to Brisbane for a week to check out properties, work opportunities and the lifestyle

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the transformation of a terraced house in Abbey Wood, south-east London, as well as a seven-year project on a large period house in Torquay

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at an antiques fair in Stamford, Lincolnshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp challenges the conkers world champion

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits the Salvation Army's Hadleigh Farm in Essex to celebrate more than 150 years of this Christian denomination, still going strong in the 21st century

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: City-dwelling families, couples and individuals set out to find their ideal rural retreats with the help of a property expert

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen is on a quest for cash, as he reviews the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. It's a story of one-armed bandits, potato rustlers and £30 pints, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Bauhaus-Galan. Gabby Logan introduces live coverage from the fourth Diamond League event of the season, which is held at Stockholm Olympic Stadium. Britain's Holly Bradshaw will be competing in the women's pole vault, up against Greece's Katerina Stefandi, who edged her out of the medals at the last World Championships. The men's event features young Swedish star - and another Monaco winner - Mondo Duplantis. With analysis by Colin Jackson

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: Iolo Williams continues his exploration of the Cambrian mountains, heading to the Tywi forest to look for goshawks. He tracks these birds of prey during the late part of the nesting season and finds fully grown chicks in a huge nest with bits of eaten prey below. Iolo also explores the Elan Valley to find the curlew, one of Wales' most critically endangered bird species, as they attempt to breed on rough ground near the reservoirs. And he discovers a stunning day-flying moth called the Welsh clearwing

programme.title: The Story of Wales
programme.desc: Huw Edwards continues his exploration of Welsh history by analysing the boom of the coal industry, and its equally dramatic demise. Steam coal from the country was world-renowned as being among the best and became known as 'black gold'. Welsh pits, attracting hundreds of thousands of migrant workers with different languages and cultures, evolved into bustling modern worlds of their own, and made a major contribution to the development of the country

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on the Somerleyton Estate in Suffolk to find out about an ambitious project aiming to turn East Anglia into a giant nature reserve. Adam Henson fears the worst for his winter barley as he gets harvesting

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from underneath the last Concorde to ever fly at Aerospace in Bristol, which is celebrating 50 years since its maiden flight. Objects brought in to be valued include a Fijian war club, a tiny Chinese vase, a collection of Radiohead memorabilia, a four leaf clover brooch, a rare piece of Venetian glass and a set of silver candlesticks

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: The general election is approaching and while Kabir remains desperate to make amends, Lata finds herself courted by Amit and Haresh in Calcutta. Maan is once again separated from Saeeda when he accompanies his father on the campaign trail to the Nawab's village seat in Rudhia. Andrew Davies' adaptation of Vikram Seth's novel, starring Tanya Maniktala

programme.title: The Notebook (2004)
programme.desc: An elderly man reads a love story to a woman in a nursing home, from an old notebook full of sentimental scribblings about a passionate young couple whose fledgling affair is smothered almost before it has begun. Romantic drama, starring Ryan Gosling, Rachel McAdams, Gena Rowlands and James Garner

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Sally Nugent

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Yana the tiger cracks a canine tooth and it must be removed, but it is not a simple procedure and the anaesthetic does not go to plan. Kate Humble and Ben Fogle present

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore a two-bed terrace in Salford, a three-bed semi in Derbyshire and a one-bedroom flat in south London

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value to take to auction. Away from the contest, Charlie also meets a familiar face with a passion for Georgian glass

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine suffers an indignity at a gig, and Shannon reacts badly to rejection

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: TV doctors David Bull, Ellie Cannon, Linda Papadopoulos, Ranj Singh and Javid Abdelmoneim take on the general-knowledge champions to win a cash prize for charity. Jeremy Vine asks the questions

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is in Cornwall, on hand to help a couple from London make their first joint property purchase with a budget of £750,000. Margherita also makes time to help out the head gardener at Trebah, one of the Great Gardens of Cornwall

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: A window cleaner, a children's party entertainer and a train supervisor compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each spending a day working at the other's jobs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell go in search for items in Cumbria, setting out from Cockermouth and heading towards an auction near Kendal

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball presents the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe has been held prisoner in Iran for more than four years after being convicted of spying charges. Nobody knows how many other British citizens have been imprisoned by the Iranian authorities, because the UK government refuses to say. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre meets the families of some of those who have been arbitrarily detained and asks whether the payment of a historic debt could set them free.

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Linda Henry and Luisa Bradshaw-White, aka sisters Shirley and Tina Carter, who talk about their characters' most memorable moments and revealing some of their best behind-the-scenes stories. Linda chats about how fans react to seeing 'Shirley' in the street, Luisa recalls her own brush with musical superstardom, and the pair explain how a tender scene quickly became a riot of laughter and chaos every time the cameras cut

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: A Friday evening trip out in the car ends in a fatal crash, but the driver refuses to accept his driving is to blame. The collision team must find the evidence to prove the real story of the night

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Maan's disgrace has a disastrous effect on the Kapoor family, but when the bad press is used against his father in the general election campaign, it results in an unexpected gesture from an old friend. Following the revelation of further interference from her meddling family and emboldened by casting her vote in India's first national democratic election, Lata makes a decision about her future. Concluding part of Andrew Davies' adaptation of Vikram Seth's novel, starring Tanya Maniktala

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: The comedy sketch show returns, pulling back the curtain on interracial adverts and discovering what happens when someone reveals spoilers for a TV series. The E19 posse do their bit for the environment, there is an episode of Jamaican Countdown, and the African aunties learn their nephew's secret. Starring Vivienne Acheampong and Samson Kayo

programme.desc: The Handmaid's Tale star Amanda Brugel is the guest host as the contestants are paired up with newcomers from LGBTQI charity Rainbow Railroad for a makeover challenge

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Bauhaus-Galan. Gabby Logan introduces live coverage from the fourth Diamond League event of the season, which is held at Stockholm Olympic Stadium. programme.title: Attenborough and the Sea Dragon
programme.desc: The naturalist joins a team of scientists examining the 200 million-year-old fossilised remains of an ichthyosaur, a vast sea creature which was discovered on Dorset's Jurassic Coast. As the skeleton is reassembled, advanced imaging technology and computer animation is used to create a simulation of the sea creature as it would have appeared when it was still alive - the most detailed reconstruction of its kind ever attempted. Meanwhile, forensic investigators painstakingly examine the bones to solve the mystery of how the ichthyosaur died

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: The global pandemic has changed everyone's lives and will continue to do so for years to come. People with disabilities have faced big challenges - from loss of essential services and support, to abuse and a lack of understanding of their needs in public spaces. Ian Hamilton investigates what life under lockdown has meant for people with disabilities and asks whether their human rights are in danger as everyday life changes to deal with coronavirus
