programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Learning that their posh neighbours the Beg-Chetwyndes have an ancient claim over their land, Mike and Alison throw an upmarket dinner party to help resolve a thorny issue. But with the ghosts her only guide to high-class decorum, the evening starts badly and only gets worse - thanks to Mary's woeful catering, Mike's clueless deal-making and Robin's raucous celebration of the lunar eclipse. Elsewhere, the spooks become obsessed with a TV sitcom. Comedy, guest starring Geoff McGivern and Sophie Thompson

programme.desc: Two astronauts are left stranded in orbit above the Earth when the debris from an exploding satellite destroys their shuttle. Left drifting in space with no way to contact mission control, they begin a perilous journey to reach the safety of a space station before their oxygen supply runs out. Thriller, starring Sandra Bullock and George Clooney

programme.title: Jason Manford's Muddle Class
programme.desc: The stand-up performer and actor presents a wealth of new material about growing up 'working class' then finding, over the years, that part of him has become 'middle class'

programme.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron (2015)
programme.desc: The superhero team faces a threat from within when Iron Man creates an artificial intelligence to take their place protecting the world. The android grows resentful of his creators, and decides his mission is best accomplished by wiping out the human race. Meanwhile, two vengeful superpowered twins seek to turn the Avengers against each other. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Hemsworth, Chris Evans and Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Ben Thompson and Sally Nugent

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Angellica Bell take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Paul McDonald needs to convince his wife Christine their future lies in Australia, so they head to Brisbane for a week to check out properties, work opportunities and the lifestyle

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the transformation of a terraced house in Abbey Wood, south-east London, as well as a seven-year project on a large period house in Torquay

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at an antiques fair in Stamford, Lincolnshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp challenges the conkers world champion

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits the Salvation Army's Hadleigh Farm in Essex to celebrate more than 150 years of this Christian denomination, still going strong in the 21st century

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah goes property hunting in rural Devon with a couple who have a £350,000 budget and hope to find a home that has both a workshop and an art studio

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Bauhaus-Galan. Gabby Logan introduces live coverage from the fourth Diamond League event of the season, which is held at Stockholm Olympic Stadium. Britain's Holly Bradshaw will be competing in the women's pole vault, up against Greece's Katerina Stefandi, who edged her out of the medals at the last World Championships. The men's event features young Swedish star - and another Monaco winner - Mondo Duplantis. With analysis by Colin Jackson

programme.title: Attenborough and the Sea Dragon
programme.desc: The naturalist joins a team of scientists examining the 200 million-year-old fossilised remains of an ichthyosaur, a vast sea creature which was discovered on Dorset's Jurassic Coast. As the skeleton is reassembled, advanced imaging technology and computer animation is used to create a simulation of the sea creature as it would have appeared when it was still alive - the most detailed reconstruction of its kind ever attempted. Meanwhile, forensic investigators painstakingly examine the bones to solve the mystery of how the ichthyosaur died

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on the Somerleyton Estate in Suffolk to find out about an ambitious project aiming to turn East Anglia into a giant nature reserve. Adam Henson fears the worst for his winter barley as he gets harvesting

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from underneath the last Concorde to ever fly at Aerospace in Bristol, which is celebrating 50 years since its maiden flight. Objects brought in to be valued include a Fijian war club, a tiny Chinese vase, a collection of Radiohead memorabilia, a four leaf clover brooch, a rare piece of Venetian glass and a set of silver candlesticks

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: The general election is approaching and while Kabir remains desperate to make amends, Lata finds herself courted by Amit and Haresh in Calcutta. Maan is once again separated from Saeeda when he accompanies his father on the campaign trail to the Nawab's village seat in Rudhia. Andrew Davies' adaptation of Vikram Seth's novel, starring Tanya Maniktala

programme.title: The Notebook (2004)
programme.desc: An elderly man reads a love story to a woman in a nursing home, from an old notebook full of sentimental scribblings about a passionate young couple whose fledgling affair is smothered almost before it has begun. Romantic drama, starring Ryan Gosling, Rachel McAdams, Gena Rowlands and James Garner

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Sally Nugent

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Yana the tiger cracks a canine tooth and it must be removed, but it is not a simple procedure and the anaesthetic does not go to plan. Kate Humble and Ben Fogle present

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore a two-bed terrace in Salford, a three-bed semi in Derbyshire and a one-bedroom flat in south London

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value to take to auction. Away from the contest, Charlie also meets a familiar face with a passion for Georgian glass

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine suffers an indignity at a gig, and Shannon reacts badly to rejection

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: TV doctors David Bull, Ellie Cannon, Linda Papadopoulos, Ranj Singh and Javid Abdelmoneim take on the general-knowledge champions to win a cash prize for charity. Jeremy Vine asks the questions

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is in Cornwall, on hand to help a couple from London make their first joint property purchase with a budget of £750,000. Margherita also makes time to help out the head gardener at Trebah, one of the Great Gardens of Cornwall

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: A window cleaner, a children's party entertainer and a train supervisor compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each spending a day working at the other's jobs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell go in search for items in Cumbria, setting out from Cockermouth and heading towards an auction near Kendal

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball presents the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: Diarmuid helps Rachel Kelly in Drumbo, Co Down, who wants to get rid of an eyesore in her garden. He is joined by plant experts Paul Smyth and Colm O'Driscoll to offer advice ,and reveals whether a huge greenhouse made of reclaimed materials has made the deadline in his own garden

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Linda Henry and Luisa Bradshaw-White, aka sisters Shirley and Tina Carter, who talk about their characters' most memorable moments and revealing some of their best behind-the-scenes stories. Linda chats about how fans react to seeing 'Shirley' in the street, Luisa recalls her own brush with musical superstardom, and the pair explain how a tender scene quickly became a riot of laughter and chaos every time the cameras cut

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe has been held prisoner in Iran for more than four years after being convicted of spying charges. Nobody knows how many other British citizens have been imprisoned by the Iranian authorities, because the UK government refuses to say. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre meets the families of some of those who have been arbitrarily detained and asks whether the payment of a historic debt could set them free.

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Maan's disgrace has a disastrous effect on the Kapoor family, but when the bad press is used against his father in the general election campaign, it results in an unexpected gesture from an old friend. Following the revelation of further interference from her meddling family and emboldened by casting her vote in India's first national democratic election, Lata makes a decision about her future. Concluding part of Andrew Davies' adaptation of Vikram Seth's novel, starring Tanya Maniktala

programme.title: Inside Autism: A Tiny Bit of Magic
programme.desc: Documentary following children with autism spending a week at an intensive therapy camp where specialist therapists try to transform challenging behaviours in just seven days. Eight-year-old Abel puts everything into his mouth and his parents are terrified that one day he is going to choke or poison himself, while seven-year-old Leo won't shower or brush his teeth and fights off any attempt to cut his hair

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: The comedy sketch show returns, pulling back the curtain on interracial adverts and discovering what happens when someone reveals spoilers for a TV series. The E19 posse do their bit for the environment, there is an episode of Jamaican Countdown, and the African aunties learn their nephew's secret. Starring Vivienne Acheampong and Samson Kayo

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: The Handmaid's Tale star Amanda Brugel is the guest host as the contestants are paired up with newcomers from LGBTQI charity Rainbow Railroad for a makeover challenge

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor David Tennant takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from April 2019, with Conservative MP Johnny Mercer and comedian Zoe Lyons joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.desc: Coverage of the day's proceedings in the House of Commons including a ten minute rule bill on redundancy protection during pregnancy and maternity leave and a debate on the economy in light of the measures announced earlier by the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak

programme.desc: The Budget, Huawei, Black Lives Matter and Domestic Abuse have occupied much of Parliament's time when it hasn't been dealing with the coronavirus pandemic. Alicia McCarthy looks back at how Westminster has tackled these and other big stories

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Conservative MP for Bolsover, Mark Fletcher, making his maiden speech in the House of Commons, from Thursday 16 January

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: SDLP MP for Foyle, Colum Eastwood, makes his maiden speech in the House of Commons, from 19 December 2019

programme.title: Inside Number 10

programme.title: House of Commons

programme.title: BOOKtalk

programme.title: House of Commons

programme.title: Select Committees

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: A big storm causes a lot of damage on the island of Struay, and Uncle Matthew's home is one of the buildings damaged

programme.title: Lon le Linda

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Micawber is in need of a bath, but first Pàdraig must get the bath to the lighthouse

programme.title: Dràgonan - Rèis chun an iomaill (Dragons: Race to the Edge)
programme.desc: When the Riders uncover the location of Viggo's Dragon Auction, they must stop him from selling dragons and stealing all the gold in Berk

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Everyone should be careful today as Fearchar is attempting archery

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Anna reveals her very unusual hobby

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports

programme.title: 'San Fhuil (It's in the Blood)
programme.desc: Made from rock as old as the moon, Uist has been shaped by winds, tides and the people who have continuously worked this landscape for 5,000 years. The traditional crofting methods enable rare species of birds and flowers to thrive that are extinct elsewhere in the British Isles.

programme.title: Druthag na Hearadh (A Little Drop of Harris)
programme.desc: A few technical glitches get sorted out as the team draws on local resources and knowledge before the distillery opens for business. The first festive season sees the community centred on the distillery peat fire, and history is made when the first Harris whisky spirit is distilled

programme.title: Bonn Còmhraidh
programme.desc: Donald Morrison examines the impact the sea had on the Gaelic community during the 1970s, which was a period of change for the River Clyde. Plus, Martin MacDonald reports the herring port at Mallaig, which was one of the last of its kind on the west coast

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: The soldiers of the special forces are still trying to find their way home and a survival expert is keen to share his skills

programme.title: Puirt-adhair (Highland Airports)
programme.desc: Things are busy in Stornoway for the fire fighters, the Loganair team and Dooley in the cafe. The Highland Gliding Club is hosting the first Scottish competition of the season, and Andrew Bruce is kept on the hop fuelling a range of aircraft in Wick

programme.title: Ceol Duthchasach/Ken Burns' Country Music
programme.desc: Biographies of the trailblazers who created and shaped Country music, from the Carter Family and Bob Wills to Hank Williams, Patsy Cline and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming in remote and unspoilt lochs and rivers, this time visiting Loch Maree

programme.title: Ever Believe Me - Sgeulachd Madeleine Smith (The Madeleine Smith Story)
programme.desc: Documentary revisiting the 19th-century murder case involving Madeleine Smith, asking whether the Glasgow socialite really did poison her lover with arsenic. Presented by John Morrison

programme.title: Annie Oakley
programme.desc: Documentary about the sharpshooter, who taught herself to hunt to support her family and became a celebrity as part of Buffalo Bill's Wild West show

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Music from acclaimed band Skerryvore, performing Take My Hand at the Trad Awards

programme.desc: When Hoppity is left in the garden, Flop and Bing have to go in search of him

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Stand aside as they are diving today and it is going to be messy

programme.title: A-null 's a-nall

programme.title: Sràid nan Sgread (Scream Street)
programme.desc: Otto starts stealing zombie body parts and selling them back. However, the 'venture' is soon brought to a standstill when he is chased by a mob of angry zombies

programme.title: Seonaidh (Shaun the Sheep)
programme.desc: An escapee from the nearby prison takes refuge in the barn. However, after a short while with Shaun and the gang, prison doesn't seem such a bad option

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: A performance by innovative band Heisk at Na Trads in 2019

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Benedict Morris, Pablo Lafuente and Ryan Murphy perform at Na Trads in 2019

programme.title: Sgeul Seirbheis
programme.desc: Gaels reflect on their experience of National Service in the 1950s

programme.title: Siubhal nan Salm
programme.desc: Part one of three. Mary Ann Kennedy explores the link between Gaelic psalm singing and the gospel music of Alabama

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Young South Uist singer Ruairidh Gray performs To God Be the Glory, while Iain Alasdair MacSween from Harris explains the role of faith in his life. Presented by Iain MacKinnon

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Having made it through a very wet winter, Sweeny now focuses on new life on the croft as he heads into spring. It is one of his favourite times of the year, and it's all go as one of his sows goes into labour, but this is one that he has to keep a close eye on. He also deals with the challenges of lambing; a time of very little sleep and plenty of early starts. With over 50 already lambed he's half way there, but his early morning checks prove vital as he finds out when he needs to give help to one of his sheep which is struggling. This isn't the only sheep he has to help. Sweeny lives on one of the most rugged coastlines in Britain, and although it's beautiful it can be very dangerous for the animals as he knows all to well. He calls the local mountain rescue in to help one of his sheep distressed on the cliff edge

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Derek Knottenbelt operates on a horse with sarcoids, Fiona Crowden treats a cow that has rapidly lost weight, and Ealasaid Dick is in the midst of lambing

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Màiri pays Donneil a visit, grateful for his support when Alasdair doesn't believe her, but she receives devastating news from the prison while she's there. Donneil receives what was left to him in the will

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: The day of Pàdruig's funeral sees the arrival of some unexpected guests, preventing the day from running as smoothly as it should. Màiri returns to the woods, determined to retrace her steps from that fateful night

programme.title: Scottish Premiership Football
programme.desc: Aberdeen v Livingston. Full-match coverage of the top-flight clash at Pittodrie

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: A performance from 2015 by the Big Music Society, created by pipers John Mulhearn and Calum MacCrimmon to promote new approaches to the ancient art of Cèol Mòr or piobaireachd. At their head is revered piper Murray Henderson and they perform a memorable rendition of Lament for Hugh

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to make see-through eggs and a way of finding out what puts the fizz into cola

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Hillwalker Colin MacLeod travels to Perthshire, where he climbs Ben Lawers, which is situated between Loch Tay and Glen Lyon. He also explores the history behind the name given to the Schiehallion mountain, the place where 18th-century astronomer Nevil Maskelyne first worked out Earth's density

programme.desc: Heather Dewar travels to Barra where she draws a keen traveller, whose experiences in foreign countries have had a profound impact of her life, outlook and even her health

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Following young dancers from around Scotland as they take part in the World Highland Dancing Championships

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Donneil struggles to contain his guilt while Alasdair bumps into Claire at the prison. Màiri plans to meet Isla at Pàdruig's house, but there's no sign of Isla

programme.title: Farpaisean Chon-Chaorach (Sheepdog Trials)
programme.desc: Coverage of day three of the International Sheepdog Trial and the Supreme Championship. Presenters Donald MacSween and Catriona MacPhee introduce the best of the action from the climax of the competition

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads visit renowned chef Marcello Tully on the Isle of Skye and attempt to impress him with their culinary skills

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is auditioning for a circus troop, but he is actually in a professional wrestling ring, in the middle of a fight!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he is in the subway but actually, he's in a cave. Running into Weasel, he thinks he's lost and decides to help him find his way.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has reduced the size of the moon in order to model it in his own image. But Magoo thinks it's a dinosaur egg ready to hatch!

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: An accident at an asteroid-based mining facility sends the rock hurtling toward the Sun, with crewman Ned Tedford still on-site. Alan and Kayo deploy Thunderbird Three for the rescue mission, but when a rogue solar flare knocks out their sensors, they are forced to find a way to alter the asteroid's trajectory so that it loops around the sun instead of plummeting to its ultra-hot surface. Animated adventure, with the voices of Rasmus Hardiker and Angel Coulby

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is thinks is out trick-or-treating for Halloween. But he unwittingly robs a bank instead of Weasel.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is a part-time firefighter reservist whose rescue mission for the day is to save a cat in a tree. In reality, the cat is none other than Fizz.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Thinking he is in the middle of a work-out at his local gym, Magoo inadvertently climbs onto a delivery boy's bicycle.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike decides to let his artistic side out and sculpt a statue of Iris, but things don't go to plan.

programme.title: Buck & Buddy
programme.desc: Beetle Buck and stick insect Buddy are the best of friends who like to collect unusual and interesting things from the strange world in which they live.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Inspired by a documentary on Buckingham Palace, Bean sets out to decorate his room in royal style, and landlady Mrs Wicket suffers delusions of grandeur

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Guilt-ridden over having lost the Traveler's Tea, Cole embarks on a dangerous quest to the top of a nearby mountain in hopes of finding the legendary Traveler's Tree.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: All Ben wants to do is stop Queen Bee from robbing a bank.

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Bunni concludes that Maggie is a witch and organises a bogus event to expose her, but the tables are turned on Bunni soon she is branded as a witch.

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Bunni thinks she's got the Champs cornered when Europe's Most Haunted Hotel TV show comes to film in the B and B. Can the Champs survive a team of crack TV ghost hunters?

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by guests Roman Kemp and Vick Hope, and chefs Marcus Wareing, Jose Pizarro and Bettina Campolucci Bordi. Featuring recipes for chicken pithivier and cheesecake, and a masterclass in the best way to prepare steak

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James samples the culinary delights of Northern Ireland with friend and fellow chef Paul Rankin, visiting a wasabi farm and a honey producer. James cooks overlooking the dramatic backdrop of Slieve Gullion - the highest point in Armagh - before the pair visit Lough Neagh

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Ben Fogle tries his hand at competitive sheep shearing in north Wales and Liz Bonnin is on the Isle of Mull, spotting pods of migrating dolphins who are returning to the shores. Plus, a visit to the Sussex home of Zoe Ball, who gives a tour of her own back garden before heading off to the National Trust's Standen House for some tips on how to make her garden grow

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the fourth and final day of the 2020 Ebor Festival, including the feature race, the 3.35 Skybet Ebor. Other races at York include the 1.50 Strensall Stakes, 2.25 Melrose Handicap and 3.00 City of York Stakes. There's also action from Sandown Park, including some very competitive handicap races. Analysis by Jason Weaver, reports from Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Mick Fitzgerald, and commentary by Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shepherd

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: The five remaining acts compete for a place in the final while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the third episode, Stephen is joined by guests John Thomson, Vick Hope and Joe Swash

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the semi-final of the junior talent contest. At the end of the Battle Rounds, each of the four judges has selected three acts to stay in the contest. These 12 solo singers and double acts must now give it their all for the chance to be their mentor's choice for next week's grand final, and get the chance to win a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort. Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am make up the regular judging panel

programme.title: Licence to Kill (1989)
programme.desc: While on leave to attend the wedding of his friend, CIA agent Felix Leiter, James Bond is asked to help with a secret mission to capture notorious drug baron, Franz Sanchez. Though successful, Sanchez later escapes and seeks revenge on Leiter, killing his new bride and leaving Leiter for dead. Incensed by this, Bond refuses his next mission from MI6 to go in search of Sanchez, leaving M with no alternative but to revoke Bond's licence to kill. Now considered a rogue agent, Bond goes undercover to pose as an assassin for hire within Sanchez's drug empire, to bring him down from the inside. Spy thriller, starring Timothy Dalton, Robert Davi, Carey Lowell, Talisa Soto and Benicio Del Toro

programme.title: Virgin Media's V Festival 2020
programme.desc: Joel Dommett and Maya Jama present festival fun from Hylands Park in Chelmsford, with exclusive performances from Olly Murs, Anne-Marie and Dizzee Rascal. Each episode sees the artists surprise their fans watching the festival from their living rooms. Plus, a look back at some of the most memorable performances from V Festival

programme.title: Small Fortune
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts a celebrity version of the game show, with a team comprising Richard Madeley, his daughter Chloe, and her husband rugby star James Haskell taking on challenges in miniature settings to win £150,000 for charity

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo mistakes some kids at the park for gnomes in a magic forest. Fizz uses a remote to immobilise them, to win over their parents!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz plans on replacing the statue from an Oscar-like ceremony with a statue in his effigy. But Magoo grabs it, thinking it's for sale in a store!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's herding cattle through the desert, but is in fact on an ice floe with walruses. He takes Fizz and Weasel for cattle rustlers!

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Lady Penelope, Parker and Gordon visit the Amazon desert, where an archaeologist shows them round an ancient temple. When they all become trapped inside, the academic refuses to let them call for help, fearing for his reputation - so the trio of heroes must put their wits to the task of finding an escape route

programme.title: Mission Employable
programme.desc: Kids' documentary series looking at what it takes to become a professional at all kinds of exciting jobs. Agent Danny is back at school, but this time he's the teacher.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that Fizz is a hat he is going to wear for his romantic date with Marnie. That's bad enough, but Fizz has a very important job to do!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: At the supermarket, Magoo mixes his shopping cart up with a baby stroller. Fizz decides to save the toddler and become a media star.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks Weasel has opened a restaurant, and wants to help him improve it. He mistakes the FizzLab for a kitchen!

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Stephan has lost his keys, and in spite of Mike's offer to find them he isn't convinced the pug has what it takes.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike's worst nightmare comes true when Louise finds a terrifying old toy at the bottom of her toy trunk.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean gets involved in a marrow-growing contest with his neighbour and, as the competition hots up, he tries all sorts of weird and wonderful methods to win. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Cole awakens inside an icy cavern belonging to a legendary beast, and attempts to escape, only to discover a surprising truth.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Hex uses a miscommunication spell to drive a wedge.

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Denise Van Outen, Stephen Mulhern and Sophie Ellis-Bextor join Martin and Roman Kemp for the entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef ends his coast-to-coast exploration of the US in New York City. After a bite of pizza at a popular pizzeria in Greenwich Village, James cooks on one of the city's famous piers. New York has a wealth of French fine-dining restaurants, and the chef cannot wait to take a bite of the action. He chooses his favourite pair, Daniel, run by his old mate Daniel Boulud, and Le Bernadine, where Eric Ripart is in charge. At the end of the day, James cooks back on the pier as sunset approaches and he reminisces on his journey of more than 3,000 miles

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the doors of British factories to find out how they keep shelves stocked, from massive factories making meals in their millions to family firms trading on secret recipes. Tonight's edition celebrates a few British classics, including a family firm that has been churning out Stilton cheese for three generations. Cameras also go behind the scenes of Lea & Perrins to see the making of their world-famous Worcestershire Sauce, as well as coffee-roasting at Taylors of Harrogate

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher's walk around the Wales Coast Path reaches Carmarthen Bay and the Gower Peninsula, an area known for its peaceful estuaries, sandy beaches and limestone cliffs. At Pendine Sands he hears the story of Wales' land-speed record holder and sees his original car roar up the beach once more. Sean also tries to beat the tide and reach the end of Worm's Head, the westerly tip of Gower

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the semi-final of the junior talent contest. At the end of the Battle Rounds, each of the four judges has selected three acts to stay in the contest. These 12 solo singers and double acts must now give it their all for the chance to be their mentor's choice for next week's grand final, and get the chance to win a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort. Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am make up the regular judging panel

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the second episode, Stephen is joined by guests John Thomson, Vick Hope and Joe Swash

programme.title: A View to a Kill (1985)
programme.desc: James Bond clashes with a megalomaniac international businessman plotting a global takeover of the computer industry by engineering a devastating earthquake in California's Silicon Valley - and killing millions in the process. Spy adventure, starring Roger Moore in his final appearance as 007 alongside Christopher Walken, Grace Jones, Tanya Roberts and Patrick Macnee

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Mark Billingham, Aldo Zilli, Lucy Pargeter and Joey Essex pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Mamma Mia! (2008)
programme.desc: A former member of a singing trio, now running a hotel on a Greek island, is reunited with her old bandmates for her daughter's wedding - but is surprised when three of her old flames also show up unexpectedly. The three men have been invited by the bride, who wants to find out which one of them is her father. Musical comedy, with a soundtrack of Abba songs, starring Meryl Streep, Pierce Brosnan, Julie Walters, Colin Firth, Stellan Skarsgard and Amanda Seyfried

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Saint's bones are discovered during an archaeological dig, causing a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. However, when the dig's leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are presented with a complex investigation as they hunt for the perpetrator. As the duo unearth more evidence, they soon discover that it is not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Fiona Dolman and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: Virgin Media's V Festival 2020
programme.desc: As the UK faces a summer of cancelled festivals and gigs, Virgin Media's V Festival returns with a virtual twist. Joel Dommett and Maya Jama present festival fun from Hylands Park in Chelmsford, with performances by Olly Murs, Anne-Marie and Dizzee Rascal. Featuring exclusive interviews, festival fashion and food, and a look back at some of the event's most memorable performances

programme.title: When English Football Ruled Europe
programme.desc: A look at English clubs' dominance of European football between 1977 and 1984. The first of two programmes looks at Liverpool's back-to-back European Cup triumphs in 1977 and 1978, and Nottingham Forest emulating the feat the following two years

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Jason Manford narrates a look at memorable days out and holidays from the soap's archives, revisiting the first episode filmed entirely on location in 1967 and a coach trip to Windermere in 1969 that ended in disaster. Plus, romantic flings of Bet, Rita and Mavis on a girl's holiday to Majorca in 1974

programme.title: Coronation Street Icons
programme.desc: Narrator Sally Lindsay focuses on Coronation Street's legendary characters, beginning with its longest serving resident, Ken Barlow. There's a look at the best moments of William Roache's six decades in Weatherfield, from the show's first episode in 1960 right up until the present day. Chris Gascoyne (Peter) and Kate Ford (Tracy) reveal how William thrives on physical scenes, and the actor himself admits his passion for an on-screen punch up, and how he has always tried to make them look as real as possible

programme.title: Emmerdale Family Tree
programme.desc: A look back at three of the soap's most-loved families, weaving its way through each family tree to reveal the loves and losses that the parents, sibiings and children have faced over five decades of drama. Up first are the Dingles, who first hit the screens in the mid-1990s. Led by bare-knuckle fighter Zak, they were not exactly nice people, but eventually they became popular with viewers and became the scheming family they are today. Gaynor Fay narrates this look back at the life of Zak, his three wives and his seven children

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Donington Park, with racing from Round Two of the Ginetta GT4 Supercup and Round Two of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team head to Blackpool, where items on Tim Hogarth, Jo Brayshaw and James Layte's respective radars include a dancing girl, Indian silverware and a Bonzo money box

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five members of an operatic society from Sheffield calling themselves the Five Tenors get their chance to take on the Tower and answer a series of Top 10 list questions for a big cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Belle shocks the Dingles, Vinny hits a nerve and Tracy puts pressure on Nate

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Peter gets in the way of Abi's morphine fix, and, desperately worried about Todd, Sean searches various social media sites hoping for clues - but is left frustrated. Nicky puts Daniel in touch with another escort

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The naked bodies of three men are found placed in the shape of a triangle, leading Jack to suspect the work of a satanic cult. Meanwhile, press baron James Callum's plans to make his mark on Denton are hindered by an investigation into his lover's death in a houseboat fire - and the suspicious demise of his former girlfriend may be the key to unmasking the killer. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Adrian Lukis, Sarah Matravers and Paul Shane

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: In 2019, the airport is having its busiest year to date, and are expecting more than 81 million passengers to fly in and out of the terminals, with its 76,000 staff ready to take on whatever is thrown at them. Officers Terry and Dave deal with an unruly passenger threatening to close the airport, while ramp team member Rachel is dishing out safety fines, and in Terminal 2 Security, Estefania is speechless with someone's carry-on luggage

programme.title: When English Football Ruled Europe
programme.desc: A look at English clubs' dominance of the European Cup between 1977 and 1984. The second programme looks at the breakup of Brian Clough's hugely successful Nottingham Forest, and the re-emergence of Liverpool, who lifted their third and fourth European cups in 1981 and 1984. Plus, the incredible story of Aston Villa, who upset all the odds to claim their one and only win in the competition in 1982

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: The food writer prepares a vegan recipe for tarka daal, provides a simple guide to making chappatis, and cooks her son's favourite meal - keema aloo matter with basmati rice. In Amritsar, she visits one of the biggest kitchens in the world, while chef Gayatri Peshwaria teaches children to cook halwa, while in London she meets the owners of a London restaurant bringing a taste of Calcutta to the capital

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is auditioning for a circus troop, but he is actually in a professional wrestling ring, in the middle of a fight!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he is in the subway but actually, he's in a cave. Running into Weasel, he thinks he's lost and decides to help him find his way.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has reduced the size of the moon in order to model it in his own image. But Magoo thinks it's a dinosaur egg ready to hatch!

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: An accident at an asteroid-based mining facility sends the rock hurtling toward the Sun, with crewman Ned Tedford still on-site. Alan and Kayo deploy Thunderbird Three for the rescue mission, but when a rogue solar flare knocks out their sensors, they are forced to find a way to alter the asteroid's trajectory so that it loops around the sun instead of plummeting to its ultra-hot surface. Animated adventure, with the voices of Rasmus Hardiker and Angel Coulby

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is thinks is out trick-or-treating for Halloween. But he unwittingly robs a bank instead of Weasel.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is a part-time firefighter reservist whose rescue mission for the day is to save a cat in a tree. In reality, the cat is none other than Fizz.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Thinking he is in the middle of a work-out at his local gym, Magoo inadvertently climbs onto a delivery boy's bicycle.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike decides to let his artistic side out and sculpt a statue of Iris, but things don't go to plan.

programme.title: Buck & Buddy
programme.desc: Beetle Buck and stick insect Buddy are the best of friends who like to collect unusual and interesting things from the strange world in which they live.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Inspired by a documentary on Buckingham Palace, Bean sets out to decorate his room in royal style, and landlady Mrs Wicket suffers delusions of grandeur

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Guilt-ridden over having lost the Traveler's Tea, Cole embarks on a dangerous quest to the top of a nearby mountain in hopes of finding the legendary Traveler's Tree.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: All Ben wants to do is stop Queen Bee from robbing a bank.

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Bunni concludes that Maggie is a witch and organises a bogus event to expose her, but the tables are turned on Bunni soon she is branded as a witch.

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Bunni thinks she's got the Champs cornered when Europe's Most Haunted Hotel TV show comes to film in the B and B. Can the Champs survive a team of crack TV ghost hunters?

programme.desc: A former member of a singing trio, now running a hotel on a Greek island, is reunited with her old bandmates for her daughter's wedding - but is surprised when three of her old flames also show up unexpectedly. The three men have been invited by the bride, who wants to find out which one of them is her father. Musical comedy, with a soundtrack of Abba songs, starring Meryl Streep, Pierce Brosnan, Julie Walters, Colin Firth, Stellan Skarsgard and Amanda Seyfried

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Saint's bones are discovered during an archaeological dig, causing a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. However, when the dig's leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are presented with a complex investigation as they hunt for the perpetrator. As the duo unearth more evidence, they soon discover that it is not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Fiona Dolman and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: Virgin Media's V Festival 2020
programme.desc: As the UK faces a summer of cancelled festivals and gigs, Virgin Media's V Festival returns with a virtual twist. Joel Dommett and Maya Jama present festival fun from Hylands Park in Chelmsford, with performances by Olly Murs, Anne-Marie and Dizzee Rascal. Featuring exclusive interviews, festival fashion and food, and a look back at some of the event's most memorable performances

programme.title: When English Football Ruled Europe
programme.desc: A look at English clubs' dominance of European football between 1977 and 1984. The first of two programmes looks at Liverpool's back-to-back European Cup triumphs in 1977 and 1978, and Nottingham Forest emulating the feat the following two years

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Jason Manford narrates a look at memorable days out and holidays from the soap's archives, revisiting the first episode filmed entirely on location in 1967 and a coach trip to Windermere in 1969 that ended in disaster. Plus, romantic flings of Bet, Rita and Mavis on a girl's holiday to Majorca in 1974

programme.title: Coronation Street Icons
programme.desc: Narrator Sally Lindsay focuses on Coronation Street's legendary characters, beginning with its longest serving resident, Ken Barlow. There's a look at the best moments of William Roache's six decades in Weatherfield, from the show's first episode in 1960 right up until the present day. Chris Gascoyne (Peter) and Kate Ford (Tracy) reveal how William thrives on physical scenes, and the actor himself admits his passion for an on-screen punch up, and how he has always tried to make them look as real as possible

programme.title: Emmerdale Family Tree
programme.desc: A look back at three of the soap's most-loved families, weaving its way through each family tree to reveal the loves and losses that the parents, sibiings and children have faced over five decades of drama. Up first are the Dingles, who first hit the screens in the mid-1990s. Led by bare-knuckle fighter Zak, they were not exactly nice people, but eventually they became popular with viewers and became the scheming family they are today. Gaynor Fay narrates this look back at the life of Zak, his three wives and his seven children

programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker (My Family)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An American female bishop visiting St Gerard's College is found dead after drinking poisoned wine, and when another two killings occur, both mirroring macabre murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy, it appears the culprit is targeting candidates in the election to become vice-regent. However, when Innocent learns one of the suspects is hiding a dark secret, Lewis and Hathaway realise the motive is much more twisted. Guest starring Sian Phillips and Ronald Pickup, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover a dead body surrounded by rabbits that have escaped from a local pet show, after someone deliberately freed them from their cages. At the crime scene, DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover that the victim died from a stab wound to the neck, and theorise that the murderer might be a competitor sabotaging the Belville Small Pet Show, and question the Best in Show defending champion Timothy Benson. The pair must work out if this is a sign of rivalry taken too far, or is the killer driven by something darker?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The life of an eccentric and reclusive couple comes under police scrutiny when a social services investigator is killed, and Barnaby must unearth generations of family secrets and decipher astronomical charts to find the murderer. Meanwhile, the detective's wife Sarah (Fiona Dolman, The Royal Today) arrives in Midsomer as the new head of a local school. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with Phyllida Law, Edward Fox and Neil Pearson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When the actress's more famous sister Diana returns after a 40-year family rift, the body count rises, with each crime reminiscent of a movie plot - giving Barnaby and Jones what looks like a disturbing case of life imitating art. Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes star, with Harriet Walter and Sinead Cusack

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A body is found with a note reading 'They Must Be Destroyed' - a chilling reminder of a case Brooks was involved in six years previously, when extreme racist Andrew Dillon killed three boys from ethnic minorities. Steel is then put under intense scrutiny when it is revealed that his success in convicting Dillon was largely due to selective prosecution. Bradley Walsh and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate a hospital department where three patients have died in suspicious circumstances in six months - the latest being a woman brought into A&E with flu-like symptoms. The detectives learn she died after being given a lethal cocktail of prescription drugs - but with an unsigned patient chart and an extremely busy evening on the ward, tracking down the killer proves challenging. Crime drama, guest starring James Fox, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a toddler goes missing from a high-street merry-go-round, his distraught mother reports him kidnapped and a massive police hunt is launched to track him down. However, with more than one suspect and no physical evidence, the search throws up more questions than answers - especially as the boy's mum seems to be hiding something. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The Regent's Canal runs through the heart of London linking the docks with the rest of Britain. John navigates the history-filled route, finding the East End's haunted Ragged School, and making and sampling a bottle or two of 'mother's ruin' along the way. The veteran broadcaster even finds time to stop off to feed the giraffes at London Zoo.

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike attempts to earn some cash without Sid's interference. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: On the day of Gary's wedding, David tells him he needs to make a decision about whether he loves Sarah or Maria. Tim informs Geoff that he and Sally are going away to stay with Gina, and Gemma announces that she's going to set up an online mother and baby group

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi has an accident, and an oblivious Seb thinks his mum has let him down as the twins prepare to leave for Australia. Meanwhile, Paul is disappointed with Billy over his 30th birthday plans, and Mary and Sean are shocked to discover a letter from Todd to Eileen demanding money

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Seb is shocked to discover Abi is in hospital, and Mary, Sean and Billy stake out Victoria Garden, waiting for Todd to approach the money bag. While Gemma tries to keep Paul's party a secret, Daniel's obsession with Nicky continues, much to her discomfort

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Malone intimidates Dawn and tempts her to take her own life by overdosing on heroin. An anguished Gabby opens up to Leyla about how lonely she feels, and Nate's angry when Al fires him for not attending a meeting

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie reveals everything that's happened between him and Belle to Kim over the phone. When Nate arrives, ready to plead for his job, they're left wondering how much he overheard. Elsewhere, Vinny tells his father that they'll be able to subtly convince Mandy to get back together with him

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie offers Nate his job back, plus a bonus if he agrees to keep the secret. Dan is panicking as he tries to keep his financial troubles a secret from Amelia, and Leyla offers Gabby an apprenticeship at Take a Vow

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Coverage of races from Sandown Park, Cartmel and Naas, including the feature meeting of the day at Sandown, where leading juveniles line up for the Solario Stakes. Also on the card is the Atalanta Stakes, while there are two races over the jumps at Cartmel, plus two competitive races at Naas in Ireland. Presented by Oli Bell, with analysis from Matt Chapman, Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Angus, a member of the Bleakridge Watch, a group of villagers who walk the streets reporting anyone who falls foul of the law, is found dead in his meat freezer. Barnaby and Winter meet Angus's devastated sister and her daughter and learn that Angus was investigating a recent spate of burglaries in the village, but had yet to identify the culprit. Angus' post-mortem reveals strange restraining injuries on his wrists and the plot thickens as Barnaby discovers just how far some people will go for power. Frances Barber guest stars as the leader of the Watch

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Steve from Blackpool searches for his birth mother who gave him up for adoption when he was a baby. After her partner tragically died when she was only 17-years-old, she had no choice but to try and find a new family for her son, and put an advert in the local paper to try and find him the best possible parents. Meanwhile, Corine searches for the son she gave up for adoption when she was just 15-years-old, but fears he may not forgive her once he finds out she has since adopted two other children

programme.title: The Queen's Green Planet
programme.desc: Documentary following Her Majesty the Queen and an ambitious legacy project to create a global network of protected forests, spanning the 53 countries of the Commonwealth. The film also features a conversation between the Queen and Sir David Attenborough in the gardens of Buckingham Palace last summer, with the subjects ranging from climate change to conkers and birthday gifts. There is also footage of the members of the royal family involved so far in making the Queen's Commonwealth Canopy a reality, including Prince Harry planting trees in the Caribbean and Prince William in Canada's Great Bear Rainforest

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Tensions between Tom and his labourers erupt during the Sanditon cricket match. Charlotte and Sidney continue to clash, resulting in Miss Lambe being placed in terrible danger, while Lord Babington offers Esther a glimpse of a better future. Period drama, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Kris Marshall, Crystal Clarke and Charlotte Spencer

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: The battle of Waterloo gets underway, with George fighting in the thick of it. Amelia fears for her husband's life, while Jos is far more worried about himself, despite being miles from the front line. Becky sees an opportunity to make a profit from the chaos of war by selling Rawdon's horses - knowing that the cowardly Jos will pay her anything for a way to escape

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A call girl is found murdered at an upmarket apartment, apparently the victim of a client who risks losing everything if his secret obsession with the woman is revealed. Leading the cross-examination, Phillips begins to suspect someone had a reason to frame the accused, and doggedly pursues the truth. Guest starring Greg Wise, with Bradley Walsh and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The start of a shooting rampage through the streets of London leaves a nightclub peppered with bullets and two revellers dead. With the media swarming all over the case and a hostage to track down, Brooks and Devlin race against the clock to find the duo responsible for the massacre, but their defence barrister (Lesley Manville) claims one of them was more innocent victim than violent killer. With Brooke Kinsella

programme.title: Davina McCall: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: The TV presenter examines how animals survive in hostile environments by visiting some of the hottest, coldest, deepest and wettest places on Earth to experience life in extreme conditions. She begins in the deserts of Namibia in south-west Africa, and discovers it home to an incredibly diverse range of life despite the high temperatures and lack of water. Davina goes head-to-head in a running race against a cheetah, camps in the sand dunes overnight and stays with San bushpeople to learn the skills that they have developed to survive

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The age gap between Sid and his children becomes apparent when he returns home early from work. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Malone intimidates Dawn and tempts her to take her own life by overdosing on heroin. An anguished Gabby opens up to Leyla about how lonely she feels, and Nate's angry when Al fires him for not attending a meeting

Elsewhere, Vinny tells his father that they'll be able to subtly convince Mandy to get back together with him

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie offers Nate his job back, plus a bonus if he agrees to keep the secret. Dan is panicking as he tries to keep his financial troubles a secret from Amelia, and Leyla offers Gabby an apprenticeship at Take a Vow

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally asks Mike for a job as a trainee machinist. The Council agrees to rename Coronation Street after Alf. Steve is pleased to hear Fiona and Tony have split

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of Kim's funeral arrives. The Dingles kidnap one of the thugs and hold him hostage. Biff and Linda are shocked to find an abandoned baby on their doorstep

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff and Linda take the abandoned baby to the police. DI Cooke questions Zoe about the cufflink. Sophie clashes with Chris after he gets drunk

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Crane and Merton have their hands full when a sniper strikes during a clay-pigeon shoot, and Bellamy's fiery temper only serves to make matters worse. Elsewhere, Vernon decides to venture into the B&B market - but his cooking skills leave a lot to be desired. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean spies on Sid when his bad timekeeping makes her suspicious of his activities. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean is delighted when Sid actually agrees with her for once, but their plans for the day end in disaster. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: As the Ropers settle into their new home, George realises he is going to have to look for a job. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Liz Merrick is mortified when one of her patients dies of an insulin overdose, but even more so when Sgt Merton is forced to investigate her responsibility. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps embarks on a mission to make Aidensfield sparkle with his own brand of cleaning products. Drama, starring Aislin McGuckin and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank cannot believe that Zoe doubts his innocence. Kim's solicitor arrives for the reading of her will which throws up some surprises for Zoe and Chris

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Glovers are stunned to hear that Emma is the mother of the abandoned baby. Zoe finds Chris feeling sorry for himself. The Windsors arrive home to find Kelly has left with Tom

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: In an attempt to stop Sally working, Kevin tells her he will buy into the garage. Alf plans a party for the residents to announce the renaming. Maxine makes a pass at Tony

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tony and Maxine spend the night together. Tricia thinks Sally is spying on her for Mike. Kevin is worried that the garage is going to go under and he will lose his job

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Liz Merrick is mortified when one of her patients dies of an insulin overdose, but even more so when Sgt Merton is forced to investigate her responsibility. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps embarks on a mission to make Aidensfield sparkle with his own brand of cleaning products. Drama, starring Aislin McGuckin and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Judi Dench's Wild Borneo Adventure
programme.desc: Part one of two. The award-winning actress embarks on a trip of a lifetime to uncover the lush rainforests, magical wildlife and spectacular coastlines of Malaysian Borneo. Judi experiences the rainforest for the first time in a thrilling 100-mile helicopter ride to the heart of the island with expert Glen Reynolds, before observing orangutans in the wild and learning how they are helping to prevent global warming

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look back at TV comedy from 1979. It was a pivotal year for Britain, with a new prime minister in Margaret Thatcher and mass strikes leading to the so-called 'Winter of Discontent'. Television saw a return to political satire, with the BBC series Not the Nine O'Clock News, though the northern club scene was dominating comedic output at the time with the likes of The Cannon & Ball Show and the stand-up series The Comedians. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are mystified by the murders of a popular couple who were stabbed to death while they slept. Lacking any leads to possible grievances held or enemies made, the detectives suspect a case of mistaken identity, and the previous owner of the house - a banker caught up in a hedge-fund scandal - emerges as the more likely target. Guest starring Samuel West

programme.title: Prime Suspect 1973
programme.desc: Crime drama prequel following the early career of Jane Tennison, as she starts out as a single-minded 22-year-old probationary officer in 1970s London, where chauvinism and rule-bending are the norm. Jane is thrown into a brutal murder inquiry when the body of a teenage girl, Julie-Ann Collins, is found in the underground car park of a Hackney housing estate, while the family of local crime matriarch Renee Bentley are shaken by a violent attack on her. Stefanie Martini steps into the shoes of Helen Mirren to bring the character back to the small screen, with Sam Reid, Blake Harrison and Alun Armstrong also starring

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Comedy series. When a storm makes him homeless again, Wilfred has to find a new dwelling

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid suffers temporary amnesia, but Jean is on hand to produce reminders of his antics at the annual Boy Scouts reunion. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: On the day of Gary's wedding, David tells him he needs to make a decision about whether he loves Sarah or Maria. Tim informs Geoff that he and Sally are going away to stay with Gina, and Gemma announces that she's going to set up an online mother and baby group

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi has an accident, and an oblivious Seb thinks his mum has let him down as the twins prepare to leave for Australia. Meanwhile, Paul is disappointed with Billy over his 30th birthday plans, and Mary and Sean are shocked to discover a letter from Todd to Eileen demanding money

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Seb is shocked to discover Abi is in hospital, and Mary, Sean and Billy stake out Victoria Garden, waiting for Todd to approach the money bag. While Gemma tries to keep Paul's party a secret, Daniel's obsession with Nicky continues, much to her discomfort

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker (My Family)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An American female bishop visiting St Gerard's College is found dead after drinking poisoned wine, and when another two killings occur, both mirroring macabre murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy, it appears the culprit is targeting candidates in the election to become vice-regent. However, when Innocent learns one of the suspects is hiding a dark secret, Lewis and Hathaway realise the motive is much more twisted. Guest starring Sian Phillips and Ronald Pickup, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover a dead body surrounded by rabbits that have escaped from a local pet show, after someone deliberately freed them from their cages. At the crime scene, DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover that the victim died from a stab wound to the neck, and theorise that the murderer might be a competitor sabotaging the Belville Small Pet Show, and question the Best in Show defending champion Timothy Benson. The pair must work out if this is a sign of rivalry taken too far, or is the killer driven by something darker?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The life of an eccentric and reclusive couple comes under police scrutiny when a social services investigator is killed, and Barnaby must unearth generations of family secrets and decipher astronomical charts to find the murderer. Meanwhile, the detective's wife Sarah (Fiona Dolman, The Royal Today) arrives in Midsomer as the new head of a local school. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with Phyllida Law, Edward Fox and Neil Pearson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When the actress's more famous sister Diana returns after a 40-year family rift, the body count rises, with each crime reminiscent of a movie plot - giving Barnaby and Jones what looks like a disturbing case of life imitating art. Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes star, with Harriet Walter and Sinead Cusack

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A body is found with a note reading 'They Must Be Destroyed' - a chilling reminder of a case Brooks was involved in six years previously, when extreme racist Andrew Dillon killed three boys from ethnic minorities. Steel is then put under intense scrutiny when it is revealed that his success in convicting Dillon was largely due to selective prosecution. Bradley Walsh and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate a hospital department where three patients have died in suspicious circumstances in six months - the latest being a woman brought into A&E with flu-like symptoms. The detectives learn she died after being given a lethal cocktail of prescription drugs - but with an unsigned patient chart and an extremely busy evening on the ward, tracking down the killer proves challenging. Crime drama, guest starring James Fox, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a toddler goes missing from a high-street merry-go-round, his distraught mother reports him kidnapped and a massive police hunt is launched to track him down. However, with more than one suspect and no physical evidence, the search throws up more questions than answers - especially as the boy's mum seems to be hiding something. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The Regent's Canal runs through the heart of London linking the docks with the rest of Britain. John navigates the history-filled route, finding the East End's haunted Ragged School, and making and sampling a bottle or two of 'mother's ruin' along the way. The veteran broadcaster even finds time to stop off to feed the giraffes at London Zoo.
