So, when bride-to-be Beccie gives him free rein to plan their wedding, he decides to honour them - and not her - with nuptials based on the rare grooves of Northern Soul. Will this groom be able to deliver a swinging day, or will his bride be swinging wildly at him?

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Murray takes Erica's car privileges away, she and Barry buy a shabby second-hand van to get back at him. Meanwhile, when Adam objects to being voted 'nicest guy' in the school yearbook, he gets a suspension

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Inspired by the film Ferris Bueller's Day Off, Barry pretends to be ill so that he can avoid school and create an epic day of his own. However, his plans go awry when Adam has exactly the same idea. Charlie Sheen guest stars

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition comes from south-east London, where the couples serve up a Caribbean menu - complete with the husband's special sauce - Spanish dishes and healthy food

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Body-confident Ben's plans for a hot-tub wedding may prove to be too much for bride-to-be Hayley, who has always dreamed of a traditional Jewish wedding

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: All 11 couples move into the same building, where each has their own apartment. With some of the couples growing closer together, others face the fallout from the previous night's commitment ceremony. To add to the pressure, the experts up the ante by introducing another element to the experiment - Yes Week, where one person is put in charge of each relationship

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Lennox's boyfriend begins to feel neglected when she spends time on her new blog instead of with him. Meanwhile, Joe sets Mel up on a blind date, and in exchange he wants to take out her attractive friend Ariel. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and his father deal with unsettled family business. Amy and Rosa work a high-profile case. Terry wants to join the NYPD band

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The squad members partner up for their next Halloween Heist - unaware they have to be handcuffed to each other. But that is just the start of their problems

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The youngster starts an internet flame war, and Missy stands up to the boys on her baseball team. Elsewhere, Meemaw is unhappy when George Sr spends time with her new boyfriend

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The university makes George Sr a job offer in the hope of recruiting Sheldon. Georgie starts working for Meemaw's new boyfriend and Mary is determined to rid her home of greed

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The friends set up camp outside a cinema to get tickets for a new cut of Raiders of the Lost Ark - but find themselves resuming hostilities with Sheldon's old foe Wil Wheaton, the former Star Trek: The Next Generation actor. Meanwhile, Penny and Bernadette invite Amy to a sleepover

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny's father pays a visit and Leonard discovers she told her parents they were back together, so he plays his boyfriend role to the hilt - much to her dismay. Meanwhile, Raj gets drunk and makes a pass at Howard's girlfriend Bernadette. Guest starring Keith Carradine

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: A man discovers his kids are not quite the little angels he had always assumed they were when he starts spending more time with them after his wife goes back to work. Pilot episode of the comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam is given two tickets to a Pittsburgh Steelers game by a client, and must choose whether to take Andi or Don. Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam and Beverly decide to create a music video to help Erica's application to Julliard stand out, but the production does not go as planned. When Murray bonds with Lainey's father Bill, he learns what it means to be a real friend. Tim Meadows and David Koechner guest star

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica is accepted into a summer arts school, but Murray struggles to express his pride in his daughter. Barry takes up the role of school sports mascot, but fails to realise that students from a rival school are planning to beat up the mascot at an upcoming game

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake makes an arrest without much proof, and so has to spend the next 48 hours collecting enough evidence to keep his suspect in custody. While his co-workers are forced to help him out over the weekend, Charles judges a pie contest between Rosa and Gina, and the Captain gives advice to Terry, who has a problem involving his brother-in-law. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake's dreams come true when he gets the chance to spend the day with his hero, veteran crime reporter Jimmy Brogan, but their encounter has unexpected consequences. Meanwhile, Terry and Charles prepare Rosa for a day in court, teaching her to be less terrifying on the witness stand. Guest starring Stacy Keach

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: Dr Sturgis is furious when Sheldon accuses him of plagiarism. Mary sets out to find a suitable 'girl trophy' for Missy's end-of-season baseball ceremony, and Georgie has an awkward run-in with Veronica while on a date with new girlfriend Jana

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: George Sr accidentally puts the school librarian in hospital, so the Coopers nurse her back to health. Meemaw gives Georgie relationship advice

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard faces the difficult decision of choosing between his relationship with Priya and his friendship with Penny, while Sheldon is irritated by one of Howard's magic tricks. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is distraught when his online gaming account is hacked, so his friends decide to find out who is responsible. However, they make the disastrous mistake of confronting the culprit in person. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Hollyoaks Favourites
programme.desc: Anna Passey and Jamie Lomas look back at a classic storyline from 2016, and the dramatic Halloween Spooktacular stunt

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Yes Week continues with one person in complete control while the other must say yes to everything. An affair between two of the participants starts to heat up

programme.title: Supernanny US
programme.desc: Jo Frost offers advice to Dan and Miranda Andersen, who are constantly on guard for their family's safety as their children were diagnosed with life-threatening food allergies

programme.title: Celebs Go Virtual Dating
programme.desc: It is the final week and decision time for Chloe Ferry, Levi Davis, Pete Wicks and Shaughna Phillips as they choose who to take on the last agency group date

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: IT worker Rodney is 45 but has never had a relationship and is looking for an English rose who can change his romantic fortunes. Will executive assistant Mandy be that woman? Football coach Julian, 29, struggles to approach girls and usually asks his friends to do it for him, while his date Becca, 26, is looking for someone who is part bad boy to join her on the party scene

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: Giles unknowingly provides Glory with the materials for a dangerous spell, allowing her to create a powerful snake monster which she sends forth to find the Key. Joyce is admitted to hospital, where the doctors discover she has a brain tumour, while Riley feels increasingly isolated from Buffy, pushing him down a dark path. Supernatural adventures, with Sarah Michelle Gellar

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: A meteor crashes to Earth, bringing with it a strange creature that preys on the mentally ill - whose numbers in Sunnydale are swelling thanks to Glory. Joyce returns home from the hospital under Buffy's care, but her condition affects her mental state, making her a potential victim

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang re-creates a prom night on the roof of the apartment building when the girls decide they would like to re-enact their evenings with their current partners. However, Sheldon feels under pressure to take part in all the romantic traditions, and Howard worries about who Stuart will bring as his date. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon panics when Leonard announces he has to go into hospital for surgery, and the impending divorce of Raj's parents motivates Howard and Bernadette to work on their own marriage. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics turn their attention to shows including Peaky Blinders, The Cameron Interview, Jamie's Meat-Free Meals, The Great British Bake Off, Japan with Sue Perkins and World Chase Tag: 2019 World Championships. They also share their views on news coverage of a stolen toilet and Boris Johnson's visit to Luxembourg

programme.title: Supernanny US
programme.desc: Jo Frost offers advice to Dan and Miranda Andersen, who are constantly on guard for their family's safety as their children were diagnosed with life-threatening food allergies

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Body-confident Ben's plans for a hot-tub wedding may prove to be too much for bride-to-be Hayley, who has always dreamed of a traditional Jewish wedding

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition comes from south-east London, where the couples serve up a Caribbean menu - complete with the husband's special sauce - Spanish dishes and healthy food

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: In this bite-sized episode, Ellie and Nick Harrington are desperate to revamp their neglected kitchen and dining room in their Tunbridge Wells home. Kirstie Allsopp heads to Kent, where the couple are given a wicker class to help them create the 1930s style they want

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Graham Hartridge and Maggie Rogers have been together for 18 months and fancy an adventure. He would love to buy a place in the south-west of France, but she is nervous of taking the plunge and would prefer Devon - much closer to family and friends. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin help with their property search

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Diane Rollisson and Robert Somerville want a holiday home, but can't agree on whether it should be in Northumberland or the Midi-Pyrenees. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin show the couple places in both locations to help them decide

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: A couple want a rural holiday home, but are divided as to where. John McMahon wants to buy a place in the Llyn Peninsula in north Wales, where his rowing club is based, while his wife Elaine would prefer the warm climate and cultured atmosphere of France's Cote d'Azur. Laura Hamilton and Adrian Simpson help them to resolve the dilemma

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp goes property hunting in the trendy suburb of Heaton in Newcastle, to help Tom and Liz find a new home as they are tired of renting flats. Elsewhere, Phil Spencer is in the Tyne Valley searching for a family home for couple Michael and Sorrel, who only have two months left in their current rental property

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud investigates an innovative scheme launched by a Birmingham housing association, aimed at allowing people who cannot afford to buy their own homes to build them instead. The presenter questions the feasibility of unskilled men and women constructing habitable houses

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hunting enthusiast Hunni Hindley hosts the first party in Lincoln, basing it on a murder-mystery theme and concocting an unconventional menu in the hope of landing the £1,000 prize. There is a heated debate on hunting, and the other guests provide interesting discourse including Ricky Summers-Calvert, who has a revelation to make about his acting career

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Medical supplier Caroline Bevis hosts the second dinner party in Lincoln, hoping her Roaring Twenties-themed evening, including black-bottom Betty torte with beetroot ice-cream, will secure her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Insurance underwriter Glenn Priestly hosts the third dinner party from Lincoln, and hopes his theme of rock music legends will win over his guests. However, the tone of the evening is lowered by student Richard Summers-Calvert's inappropriate questions

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Bodybuilding drama student Richard Summers-Calvert hosts the fourth party of the week from Lincoln. He serves chicken and rice for his starter, main and dessert, but with a few twists, and entertains his guests with a game of charades after asking them to dress up as action heroes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: All-American mother and former barrister Natashka Ingham hosts the final dinner party from Lincolnshire, and hopes to impress her guests with a theme of The Great American Dream. Her dishes include 'fall off your horse black bottom pie' and 'cow tail pie' and, despite the fact conversation turns to underpants and toilets, she hopes to have done enough to win the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is Nateby Inn in Cumbria, where landlady Gaynor Walker is ready with a warm welcome. A trip to Gaynor's local brewery turns out to be a barrel of laughs when one guest beats another in a cask-rolling competition, before a broken cooker creates chaos in the kitchen the following morning

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Outfields Farm Bed and Breakfast in Ravensden, Bedfordshire, on their second stay, where proprietors Graham and Julia Ashley treat them to an afternoon of indoor sky-diving before hosting a dinner filled with surprises. The guests enjoy a good night's sleep, but one of the owners is expecting hassle at breakfast time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to At Home in Chiswick, west London, where artist Bob Osbourne appears to take a rather laid-back approach to running his B&B. On arrival, guests are pleasantly surprised by their first impressions of the communal living space-come-mini art gallery - but it's not long before they are dishing the dirt

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition draws to a close at the Ammonite Lodge guesthouse in Chard, Somerset, where owners Andrew and Clare Ashmore hope to excite their guests with a game of skittles. During the final dinner of the week, the competitors discuss their first impressions of each other, and Bob and Julia share some amusing observations with the group

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners confront their critics face to face, and the winners are announced. Nateby Inn host Gaynor takes issue with Graham and Julia's reduced marks for cobwebs. The conversation becomes heated when Outfields Farm's owners tackle Gaynor's complaints in return, and Georgie jumps to their defence. Artist Bob Osborne has a long list of grievances he wants to address, and Julia is left cringing when her previous comments rebound on her

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Natasha Cargill wants to build a home shaped like two enormous periscopes in rural Norfolk, but to obtain planning permission, she has to ensure not only that the materials are sustainable, but also agree to measure the transportation used to deliver them. If these strict criteria are not met, she won't be allowed to live there, and to add to the pressure, she has just £330,000 to spend on construction and six months to complete the project. Kevin McCloud follows her progress

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Schizophrenic Graham, 75, is brought to King's College Hospital after being stabbed in the neck by a stranger on his doorstep, and needs a CT scan to reveal the extent of his wound. Former nursery nurse Pat, 89, also arrives on the ward, having been found at the bottom of the stairs at home with no recollection of how she got there, while palliative care nurse Tracey treats 72-year-old retired postman Michael, who was recently diagnosed with lung cancer

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: On a busy Saturday night at King's College Hospital, staff have to juggle the needs of elderly patients and sick children alongside those who are drunk or violent. Paramedics bring 12-year-old Grace into resus after she falls down a flight of stairs, while Sarah and Andy are concerned for their 10-month-old son Dexter, who has a high temperature and is unresponsive. Elsewhere, clinical site manager Gordon has a series of tests as a result of senior sister Jen noticing his bloodshot eye

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal takes over as Craggy Island's new milkman, but his predecessor, local womaniser Pat Mustard, returns to exact an explosive revenge on Ted for getting him sacked. Starring Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal and Ted visit the mainland, where they bump into actor Richard Wilson - and discover the hard way that he does not take kindly to being confused with his comic alter ego Victor Meldrew. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon star

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with Sean Lock and Stephen Mangan teaming up against Noel Fielding and Fay Ripley. The Brett Domino Trio are in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim, Fuzz and the team work on a 1963 Alfa Romeo Giulia Spider that has been kept in a dusty barn in Essex after its owner's plans to restore it were interrupted by the onset of a serious medical condition

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to Hampshire to repair a 1982 VW Golf GTi Mk1 that has been kept in a garage since a road accident in 2001. However, they both have different ideas about how the finished car should look when it is returned to its owner

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog-mad executive PA Julie-Ann Rayner hosts the first dinner party in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, and hopes to impress her guests - science teacher Damian Loneragan, DJ Scott Langley, barmaid Steph Barrett and glamorous granny Carol Dolman - with a 'taste of the sea' menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Carol Dolman hosts the second dinner party in Southend-on-Sea, Essex. The glamorous grandmother hopes her scallops and roast lamb, 'dress to impress' theme and tango lessons given by local instructors are enough to win her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teacher Damian Loneragan hosts the third dinner party in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, where he hopes his chilli-based dishes, and a lemon and ginger dessert, will help him win the £1,000 prize. However, after a disastrous main course, he goes all-out to wow his guests with science experiments in the garden

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Barmaid Steph Barrett is the fourth host of this week's competition in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, and sets a 1940s dress code. The day gets off to a tricky start when she attempts to make home-made pastry for the first time, but her nan saves the day. The guests are suitably impressed by her home, and the dancing entertainment goes down even better than the food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Scott Langley hosts the final party in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, and decides to take a gamble by serving fried pigeon as his main course. He insists on an 'outrageous' dress code for the evening, and unveils some special entertainment before the week's winner is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Ian and Sam Sugden's Abbey House in Penrith, Cumbria, where the guests are impressed with the cleanliness of the rooms. However, there is some grumbling over breakfast about the bacon

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners stay at Compton House in Newark, Nottinghamshire, which is run by Lisa Holloway - who prides herself on providing a home away from home for her visitors. However, some of the guests have issues with dust and the haddock served at breakfast - and challenge whether a night's stay is worth the price tag

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave and Jo Bate welcome their guests to the White Horse, a pub and B&B in Ripon, North Yorkshire, where they hope an afternoon of morris dancing in front of the cathedral will make a lasting impression. Unfortunately, Ed and Jane Mitchell get locked inside their room by mistake, and there are arguments over tomatoes at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Retired teachers Ed and Jane Mitchell welcome the contestants to the Old Farmhouse, Cheshire, but everyone is shocked to discover they will have to share a bathroom. Meanwhile, Sam and Ian Sugden find an unwelcome surprise underneath their bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment day arrives, as the guests gather to find out who has won and to thrash out their differences - from some fishy findings to a reminder of why sorry can be the hardest word. Later on, a heated exchange about cleaning ends in tears

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: National Heritage worker Gillian Sutherland hosts the first dinner party in Inverness, and has high hopes that her guests - emergency-call handler Melissa Kirby, property tycoon David Edes, hair stylist Zarina Ainslie and personal fitness trainer Jamie Tulloch - will appreciate her traditional Scottish menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Designer-label fan Melissa Kirby hosts the second dinner party in Inverness, hoping her cooking lessons from one of the city's best chefs will win her the £1,000 prize. However, rival Gillian is unimpressed when she finds out the chef also made the gravy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Property tycoon and teetotaller David Edes hosts a nautical evening for the third dinner party in Inverness, where his menu of 'basic but wholesome food' makes his guests wary. A night of suspicion and hilarity between the diners ensues

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth night of the contest in Inverness, it is hairdresser Zarina Ainslie's turn to host. The fun-loving hopeful needs to live up to her lofty claims of being an amazing cook as her fellow contestants prepare to rate her on the evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former professional footballer Jamie Tulloch hosts the final party in Inverness, going to the trouble of ensuring all the ingredients in his chosen dishes are locally sourced. However, it remains to be seen whether his home-grown approach to fine dining will be enough to impress his discerning fellow competitors, and as the night draws to a close, the week's winner is revealed

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits Andy and Nicky Bruce, who were building an experimental amphibious house on a small island on the Thames in Buckinghamshire. They spent £1.2million on one of the most ambitious Grand Designs ever, but the logistical headache of building on an island only accessible by a narrow footbridge soon became clear. Heavy machinery was swept away by the river, contractors were changed, and the site was hit by severe storms, causing delays and resulting in an unfinished and untested building. Kevin returns to find out what living on a regularly flooded piece of land is like, and whether the floating home performed when the river burst its banks in January 2015

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Incidents involving the West Midlands Ambulance Service at night, including a fight in a fast food restaurant, and a woman who has fallen while carrying her four-month-old grandson

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Paramedics across Britain treat patients fighting for their lives. In Nottingham, Selina Conway is dispatched to a 76-year-old man who has collapsed in his garden, while Liam Wakefield heads to the home of an 18-year-old who has stopped breathing. In Essex, Sadie Bentley and recently qualified Anna Tansley are called by the coastguard to help resuscitate a man found floating in the sea

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff of King's College Hospital treat patients involved in traffic accidents. Jade from Kent is brought in after being hit by a car and knocked under a bus on her way to school, and doctors are concerned the 12-year-old may have fractured her skull. Meanwhile, Geraldine, 60, has been injured while trying to cross the road, and after recently being released from prison 40-year-old Brian is in minors with a broken ankle, but the swelling means his electronic curfew tag will have to be removed

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Incidents involving the West Midlands Ambulance Service at night, including a fight in a fast food restaurant, and a woman who has fallen while carrying her four-month-old grandson

programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique and valuable items, and their latest find is an old bureau which gets a makeover in Yorkshire

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien recycles some historic oak while Henry Cole comes unstuck when he tries to get to grips with an old Ford Escort

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul plan to host locals - including the mayor - to view the work they have done on the front of their chateau. However, Paul has first to design, build and, with a bit of advice from Dick Strawbridge, fit two heavy balconies from scratch. Elsewhere, Mariam and Johnno get their antique fireplace fitted, create a stylish sink out of a copper bucket, and host a 'thank you' meal for their builders

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Ronnie and Dana in rural Essex, who are converting an ancient barn into a stunning new home

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners of Guest and the City, Dan and Mardi Leonard, welcome their visitors in Brighton, but there is a mixed reaction to the couple's contemporary decor and stained glass windows. The unconventional furniture leaves some guests confused, and one pair of fellow hoteliers bemoan the lack of sausages at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Phil Kiernan invites the contestants to the Farmer's Boy Inn in Longhope, Gloucestershire, where he challenges them to a pie-making competition. However, the task proves less entertaining than the bickering that occurs between two guests as they try to share a kitchen

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Jane Asher host on the Geanna, their purpose-built hotel boat. Dan and Mardi Leonard are concerned space is too tight, while Sarah Langford and Jean-Pierre Kujawa worry about their privacy being invaded. A trip up the Thames allows the guests to sip champagne and get to know the ropes, but the mood at dinner is subdued, and when the owners cook their first breakfast on board they find themselves bickering

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sarah Langford and Jean-Pierre Kujawa, who have run the Sentry Mead B&B on the Isle of Wight for six years, play host in the last challenge of the week. The guests arrive with high expectations and after a few setbacks, the dinner and breakfast win everyone over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Eastern black rhino Zuri gives birth to baby Ike, and sun bears Toni and Millie are having relationship issues. Meanwhile, Bactrian camel Shan-shan is under the weather, so the Chester keepers are trying to work out how to help her

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick and his team return to provide more cutting-edge animal care. The professor's first patient is labradoodle Mitzi, who has been hit by a car - and Noel's biggest concern is that she may have suffered nerve damage, leaving her owners fearing the worst. Options are limited for 10-month-old German pointer Hector, who is struggling to walk - he must undergo spinal surgery or face the prospect of being put to sleep, while a Leonberger with a cancerous leg undergoes an operation to remove the tumour

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge steps in to help new chateau owners couple Erin and JB who, having recently moved into their fairy-tale chateau complete with turrets and a moat, have discovered rising damp. Dick goes on an exploration of their vast cellar in search of the leak

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits a memorable restoration project in the remote Creuse region of central France. Back in 2003, when Denise Daniel and Doug Ibbs gave up everything in the UK to start a new life abroad, they couldn't have known what was ahead of them. Falling in love with a dilapidated manor house with just four crumbling walls and half a roof, they embarked on a mission to transform it - working day and night, doing everything themselves, year after year - until slowly but surely they created a magnificent edifice out of the ruins they bought off the internet. On the 70th anniversary of the destruction of the original 19th-century building by the German army, the house finally took its finished form

programme.title: Inside the Zoo: Down Under

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Bologna in northern Italy, a city so famous for its food that it's known as the stomach of Italy. Later, Rick drives up into the hills to a cheese dairy where he tastes a 13-year aged Parmesan and enjoys lunch in at a pig farm with restaurant attached, where huge charcoal-grilled pork steaks are served

This time, they're up against pro driver Shane Lynch's Chevrolet Camaro in a plane-pulling contest. Building scrap able to get the 40-ton monster rolling will require innovation and a super-sized imagination.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they continue on their angling trip round the UK. In the Monsal Valley in Derbyshire, Paul tries to teach Bob how to fly fish for wild rainbow trout, and the pair reflect on their humble beginnings, their careers and their comedy contemporaries. Later, Bob cooks Paul his very personal recipe of tuna melanie on the river bank, and it soon transpires his cooking is a lot more successful than his fishing

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The desert town that faced a disaster of biblical proportions. Plus, scorched remains that speak of imprisonment and torture and vast coastal ruins that reveal a past once wiped from history

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the world's fastest driverless car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Vienna, the city that once ran the Austro-Hungarian Empire and continues to be home to comfort dishes such as tafelspitz and goulash, and which gave its name to one of Europe's most popular dishes - the Wiener schnitzel. Rick learns the essential etiquette to the Austrian capital's coffee house culture and indulges in its sweet tooth by enjoying a plate or two of apple strudel and Sacher torte. He also finds time to enjoy the traditional music of the country, typically Mozart and Strauss

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and the team take on their toughest challenge yet - keeping their tongues in check during a meeting with their counterparts from the German equivalent of the show. James May also test-drives a quirky Japanese car, and Richard Hammond puts a Mazda concept car through its paces. Jamiroquai frontman Jay Kay is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May test-drive an Aston Martin DBS Convertible, a Ferrari California and a Lamborghini Gallardo Convertible through Romania, searching for the mountain highway built during the reign of the country's former dictator Nicolae Ceausescu - allegedly the best road to drive on in the world. Actor Eric Bana is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which two teams of celebs divulge what sound like tall tales about themselves, before their rival contestants decide whether they are lying. programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which two teams of celebs try to call each other's bluff with a few stories about themselves that might be true or might be out-and-out porkies. Joining team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are comedians Jo Brand, Roisin Conaty and Paul Foot, and TV wildlife presenter Ray Mears

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Beattie Edmondson, Amol Rajan, Gaby Roslin and Hugh Dennis test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade returns with the offbeat tourism show and is joined in the first edition by comedian and Not Going Out star Lee Mack for a short break in the Belgian capital. Their two-day trip features culture, comics, pralines, puppets and attractions both large and small, including the Grand Place and the famous atom-shaped Atomium, as well as waffles, mussels - including Moules Ghenghis Khan - and the secrets of chocolate-making

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by Cold Feet star Fay Ripley for a wintry mini-break in Norway's capital, where they first head to the Thief hotel - home to an art collection including works by Warhol, Hirst, and Koons, as well as a Bryan Ferry-themed dining room. They also take a ride on the city's most popular toboggan run and try to master cross-country skiing, before visiting the National Gallery to check out Edvard Munch's masterpiece The Scream, and sampling Michelin-starred food at Kontrast

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores nature's evolutionary anomalies and examines two creatures whose stretched features have given them an edge in the natural world. The chameleon has an extra-long tongue that helps it catch prey and the giraffe's neck is so long it is easily able to reach the tops of trees for food

programme.title: Viking Dead
programme.desc: How a well-preserved Viking ship discovered in 1904 continues to present archaeologists with new mysteries to unravel

programme.title: How The Beatles Changed The World
programme.desc: Through exclusive interviews and a wealth of rarely seen footage, this film reveals the previously untold story of how four young men from Liverpool transformed the world forever

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the work of the train operating company's staff along the route between London, south-west England and south Wales. The first edition focuses on problems caused by last winter's storms, with the worst weather for a century battering the nation and pushing employees and passengers to the limit. Network Rail's Steve Hawkins and his engineers have their work cut out after the line at Dawlish in Devon is washed away, while Dave Slater and his team at Network Control have to battle the elements to keep trains running as storm after storm floods the network

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: As rugby union's Six Nations Championship kicks off in Cardiff, train manager Jodie Edwards and her team try to get thousands of fans home after the match. Penzance in Cornwall remains shut off from the rest of the network as engineers work around the clock to get services running through Dawlish, where the line was washed away, while new duty manager Joe has his hands full on his first Saturday night shift at London's Paddington station when the last Wales-bound train of the evening has a problem

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Cheltenham Spa station manager Richard Morrish braces himself for a hectic four days with the two-platform station used by more than 60,000 horse racing fans during the Cheltenham Festival. Just as the mainline at Dawlish is about to be reopened after being washed away in the winter storms, a crack is discovered in the sandstone cliffs two miles down the line and a landslide threatens to engulf the tracks. Conductor Jameil and driver Gary have to react quickly on the Reading to Gatwick service when a pedestrian takes a perilous short cut

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: The first of three programmes in which cameras explore some of the most inhospitable places on the planet and the people who live there, including a team of Sherpa roping a route on Mount Everest's notorious Khumbu Icefall and, on the slopes of Mount Kilimanjaro, farmers fight pitched battles to save their crops from marauding elephants. In the Amazon rainforest, two boys undergo a rite of passage involving hundreds of ants with the most painful stings on Earth, and conservationists in the Grand Canyon try to ensure the survival of one of America's few surviving condor chicks

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: Documentary profiling some of the world's most expansive - and inhospitable - landscapes, and the people who live there. A fisherman in Zambia faces off against crocodiles and risks being swept to his death as he tries to make a living above the imposing Victoria Falls, and in the Amazon, a giant fish offers salvation to a hungry family that calls the river home. A boy's future hangs on the outcome of a single horse race in the vast grasslands of the Steppe, and in a European salt marsh, a young man duels with a savage bull in a centuries-old battle of wills

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: A man in Borneo must provide for his family by climbing to the roof of a vast cave to collect birds' nests and a Bangladeshi father and son brave deadly bees and tigers to find honey

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: The first of three programmes in which cameras explore some of the most inhospitable places on the planet and the people who live there, including a team of Sherpa roping a route on Mount Everest's notorious Khumbu Icefall and, on the slopes of Mount Kilimanjaro, farmers fight pitched battles to save their crops from marauding elephants. In the Amazon rainforest, two boys undergo a rite of passage involving hundreds of ants with the most painful stings on Earth, and conservationists in the Grand Canyon try to ensure the survival of one of America's few surviving condor chicks

programme.desc: Documentary profiling some of the world's most expansive - and inhospitable - landscapes, and the people who live there. A fisherman in Zambia faces off against crocodiles and risks being swept to his death as he tries to make a living above the imposing Victoria Falls, and in the Amazon, a giant fish offers salvation to a hungry family that calls the river home. programme.title: How The Beatles Changed The World
programme.desc: Through exclusive interviews and a wealth of rarely seen footage, this film reveals the previously untold story of how four young men from Liverpool transformed the world forever

programme.title: Prince Philip: The Plot to Make a King
programme.desc: Secret History documentary telling the inside story of the dynastic tensions that were unleashed when Princess Elizabeth, the future queen, fell in love with Prince Philip. It was felt that Philip was rough and ill-mannered - and with the Second World War still fresh in most people's minds, the royal and political elite disliked his German roots. But following the death of King George VI in 1952, the establishment feared a dynastic coup, with the Queen torn between the two sides. The documentary examines the complexities and frustrations of the royal marriage, as the monarch struggled to reconcile the love of her husband with the suspicions of her family and her government

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The crew of HMS Queen Elizabeth completes the vessel's sea trials, testing an innovative new firefighting system for the first time and marking the 100th anniversary of a plane being successfully landed on a moving ship. The carrier returns to Portsmouth, which will be its home port for the next 50 years, while the pilots of its fighter jets go to the US for additional training

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Antiques dealer and expert restorer Gary Wallis and his team of highly skilled craftspeople turn the tired and unloved items into objects that will turn a large profit. In the opening episode, they restore a Grenadier guard's drum, create a garden feature from a helicopter tripod and re-purpose a vintage car grille into a gleaming design classic

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Wokingham in Berkshire to meet professional gardener Mark whose barns are brimful of bikes, bric-a-brac and baggage

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of a restaurant in Mount Sinai, New York, which appears to be stuck in the 1980s. The crumbling decor and unenthusiastic head chef at the Handlebar need to be tackled, but owners Bill and Carolyn LeRoy are in denial about the problems they face

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Italian family restaurant Sabatiello's in Stamford, Connecticut, where the owner's fiery temper upsets the staff and has left him more than $1million in debt. Gordon's help is urgently needed to get customers back through the door, but the restaurateur does not take kindly to criticism and closes the restaurant down when his employees turn on him

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef comes to the aid of Anthony Trobiano, whose restaurant in Great Neck, New York, is failing even though his partner's parents have given him their life savings and he has taken out half a million dollars in loans. Gordon has some harsh criticism about the unhygienic kitchen and the quality of the food being served, but the owner has trouble accepting his advice

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding present the first in a series of special charity editions of the culinary challenge, with judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith. The first set of contestants are documentary maker Louis Theroux, comedians Jenny Eclair and Russell Howard, and athlete Ovie Soko, who are challenged to make a giant biscuit, a classic bake, and a choux sculpture depicting their perfect day

programme.title: Derren Brown: 20 Years of Mind Control
programme.desc: The illusionist performs his latest psychological trick live, and also revisits some of the most memorable moments of his 20 years of making programmes for Channel 4. He reveals how he has used hypnosis and the power of suggestion to baffle the British public, before opening up about his most controversial, headline-grabbing moments, including playing Russian roulette, 'sticking' viewers to their sofas, and the zombie-fuelled Apocalypse illusion

programme.title: Derren Brown: Apocalypse
programme.desc: He survived a game of live Russian roulette, 'predicted' the lottery numbers, stuck half the viewing audience to their sofas and taught a member of the public to fly a plane overnight. Now, acclaimed showman Derren Brown presents a two-part special that is arguably his most audacious stunt yet, as he convinces one person that the planet has been devastated by a meteor strike and that he is only one of a handful of survivors. Will the chosen one rise to the challenge?

programme.title: Derren Brown: Apocalypse
programme.desc: The acclaimed showman concludes his two-part special in which he convinces one person they have survived a meteor strike on Earth, which is now a zombie-ridden land. The unwitting participant continues his adventure in a post-apocalyptic world as a series of challenges and staged situations begins to transform the way he sees himself and his life

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourites
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates recipes for simple, affordable and delicious meals, from quick and tasty dinners to useful staples to add to regular repertoires. The chef kicks off by teaming chicken with flavour-packed Cumberland sausages and tasty veggies to creating a one-pan roast dinner, and puts a British twist on the classic spaghetti Bolognese by swapping in pale ale and Cheddar cheese to create an easy, cheap and tasty batch-cook recipe. Plus, he's got a fun take on pasta preparation that the kids - and adults - will love

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Canal boat stewardess Denise Hendon hosts the first dinner party in Birmingham. Despite admitting her cookery skills could be better, she hopes her night of Moroccan-themed revelry will be a success, but the guests are underwhelmed by her menu. While party girl Angela McFarlane dreads eating the couscous, suave Frenchman Jean-Benoit Burloux longs for a glass of fine wine to wash the meal down

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Daniela Lawler throws the second party in Birmingham, hoping to impress her guests by inviting them to the Georgian country house she shares with her husband, DJ Steve Lawler. The hostess aims high by planning a four-course Italian-inspired meal, and everything seems to be going well, until she realises her lobster is too big to fit in her pans

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Office administrator Angela McFarlane hosts the third dinner party in Birmingham, and hopes to impress with a starter of chicken and spring onion terrine with red onion marmalade. However, the guests seem more interested in rummaging through her bedroom drawers

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Electrician Elliot Jauncey hosts the penultimate dinner party in Birmingham, and despite a lack of experience, plans a top-class menu featuring two desserts. He also books a local band to serenade his guests, but the evening turns out to be far more difficult than he had expected

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jean-Benoit Burloux hosts the final dinner party in Birmingham, and plans a four-course menu including crab in a bird's nest, a blade of beef and a pavlova with raspberries. However, the cook has to find a way of overcoming Denise's fussy eating habits before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin shows five properties to Rachel and Miguel, who have sold their house in Dorset and are now planning a permanent move to Spain's highly sought-after Orihuela Costa

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps a couple find a holiday home on the Greek island of Crete, hoping to find a traditional village house on a budget of £80,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps a family from North Shields search for a property in the picturesque coastal town of La Duquesa on Spain's Costa del Sol

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Nurse Karen and GP Alastair from Montrose long to relocate to the beautiful countryside of the Dordogne in France. Laura Hamilton shows them five options for their £175,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Kamlesh and Sukhwinder have been married for 42 years, and now their four children have flown the nest, wish to buy a winter home in southern Alicante, with its breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea and lively atmosphere. Jean Johansson shows them five properties for their £95,000 budget, but whether any will take their fancy remains to be seen

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The owners of Jack's Waterfront at St Clair Shores in Michigan are keen to hear the fiery chef's advice for turning their failing seafood restaurant around. The lakeside location is a popular summer spot with fantastic views, but an uninspiring menu and a manager more skilled at socialising than organising are preventing them from achieving year-round success

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay visits family-run Luigi's in Orange County, California, where lazy shortcuts following the retirement of the owner's father have led the business to suffer. Ramsay faces the daunting task of reuniting a feuding family, before turning around the struggling restaurant, which currently has 126 dishes on the menu

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of the owners of Hannah & Mason's, a French bistro in Cranbury, New Jersey. Members of staff complain their pay cheques bounce, and the kitchen cooler is so unhygienic that Gordon makes the decision to close the restaurant on Valentine's Day - the busiest night of the year

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to the Park's Edge restaurant in Atlanta, Georgia, where a series of spats with the local community are hampering its success. Owners Richard and Jorge's in-fighting has alienated locals, and the duo's suggestion that customer numbers are low because of racism has heightened tensions

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to the Greek at the Harbour restaurant in southern California, where owner Makis Mikelatos works 17 hours a day, seven days a week, but is unwilling to let his son Ari take the reins. Gordon sets out to get to the bottom of Makis's reluctance to teach his son, and uncovers a shocking family secret

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Mike & Nellie's Steakhouse in Oakhurst, New Jersey, where owner Mike Farber is mourning his father Nellie, who was also his business partner. Having been called in by the restaurateur's two daughters, Gordon faces the task of getting him to focus on his business, before tackling the bigger problem of rotting decor and a less-than-appealing menu

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer visit the Payne-Durkins, on the rural outskirts of Leeds, to help Karl and Paul decide whether to renovate or sell their three bed home. Karl loves the unique design and idyllic location, but Paul thinks they are too isolated, and would rather move to another area than spend the moving improving their current home

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: A compilation of the best bits from recent series with host Jimmy Carr, team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, and Countdown regulars Susie Dent and Rachel Riley, joined by a host of top comedians including Alan Carr, Richard Ayoade, Kathy Burke, Roisin Conaty, Joe Wilkinson, Johnny Vegas, Katherine Ryan, Phil Wang, Aisling Bea and David Mitchell

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Daisy May Cooper take on guest team captain Richard Ayoade and Ivo Graham. Adam Buxton is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, and Joe Wilkinson joins Rachel Riley at the numbers board

programme.title: George Clarke's National Trust Unlocked
programme.desc: George discovers some of the UK's most impressive historic homes, as he is granted special access to some of these extraordinary places, delving into the hidden recesses that visitors don't normally get to see. In this first episode, George's journey begins in Dorset at the UK's most exquisite example of Italianate architecture - the magnificent 17th-century Kingston Lacy. He uncovers a tale of shame and scandal on the south coast, and the beautiful Cotswold landmark - Hidcote Manor Gardens - in full bloom takes his breath away. George also mines the history of the country's last cave dwellers at one of Britain's best-kept secrets, the Rock Houses at Kinver Edge, and finally, with his husky Loki, takes a walk at spectacular Studland Bay, part of the Jurassic Coast World Heritage Site

programme.title: Lose a Stone in 21 Days with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: The doctor puts five volunteers on a low-calorie diet to help get their health back on track after the UK's Covid-19 lockdown. In the third and final week of the diet challenge, Michael explores the connection between comfort eating, and the different ways our brains respond when faced with a bar of chocolate as opposed to a pile of fruit and vegetables. He also has the unique experience of having a camera stuck up his nose to learn about the connection between weight, snoring and the life-limiting condition of sleep apnoea

programme.title: Puppy School
programme.desc: With four pre-school children, life is proving hectic for Cambridgeshire mum Ella, who urgently needs advice on controlling boisterous Labrador puppy Luna's anxiety issues. Teachers Julie and Richard hoped their cocker spaniel puppy would fill the empty nest now their sons are leaving home, though making the puppy the centre of attention has created problems. The Chinery family from Berkshire arrives at Puppy School looking for tips with managing Benji - the cockapoo they hoped would provide much-needed fun for their daughters after the tragic death of their baby sister

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at The Admiral Hotel in central London, run by owner So Shaw and hotel manager Kevin Jankunas. Chris Foster and his German companion Christel Richter are floored by the London prices, and Ursula Beswick and Daria Pakalnicka question the quality of the facilities. Despite So and Kevin's best efforts to charm their guests, a dinner conversation leaves Kris Malde and Vikki Cheatham making accusations of sexism and So and Kevin are outraged by some of their feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to The Redstone in Blackpool, where high-end hotelier Ursula Beswick and manager Daria Pakalnicka hope their luxury guest house will win the day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Chris Foster and Christel Richter invite the hoteliers to Ann's Farmhouse in the suburbs of Bradford, West Yorkshire, but tensions remain high between So, Kevin, Kris and Vikki

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the R Inn in Desborough, owned by Kris Malde. However, when simmering tensions between the guests boil over, one of them refuses to stay the night

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: As the B&B owners meet for the last time, bones of contention include a missing biscuit, marks on a toilet seat and one guest is confronted about why they refused to stay the night. There are harsh words and home truths before the overall winner is revealed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Part-time model and writer Azzi Kalili hosts the first dinner party in west London. She erects a Moroccan marquee in her garden, but seems oblivious to her freezing guests and their dislike of her food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Plumber Steve Capel hosts the second party in west London, putting together his favourite dish, a roulade of ostrich, kudu and springbok. However, his plans to entertain the other diners with a demonstration of his nunchuk skills are cut abruptly short when an injury sustained in the kitchen comes back to haunt him

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ex-wedding planner and self-confessed perfectionist Zae Greenwood hosts the third dinner party in west London. He tries to impress his guests with a wedding-style menu, but they do not respond well to his culinary efforts

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Events organiser Emma Boardman Magliocchetti hosts the penultimate dinner party in west London. She hopes to prove to her new Italian husband that she can be the perfect wife and create an incredible feast, despite never having cooked a three-course meal before

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jane Welch hosts the final dinner party in west London, and has asked her guests to appear in fancy dress. The busy mother is initially nervous about the evening and her adventurous menu, but becomes emotional when her diners compliment the meal. Plus, the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Nurse Karen and GP Alastair from Montrose long to relocate to the beautiful countryside of the Dordogne in France. Laura Hamilton shows them five options for their £175,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Bettina and Eamon have spent nearly 30 years in military accommodation moving with Eamon's job. They are now ready to start the next chapter of their lives by buying the first property they will have bought together. They have chosen the Costa Calida in Spain and are hoping one of the five properties Danni Menzies has found within their £100,000 budget prove be perfect for them

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Hero in Burnham Overy Staithe, Norfolk, which is run by two friends who quit university to follow their dream of becoming hospitality entrepreneurs

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole visit Witney in Oxfordshire, where Henry comes across a tractor that has not run for quite a few years - but poses no problem for restorer Alan

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Seattle shrink takes a dislike to KACL's newest presenter, sharp-tongued radio psychiatrist Dr Nora. Award-winning episode of the comedy series, guest starring Christine Baranski and Piper Laurie

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Cassandra Stone, the woman Frasier dated on Valentine's Day, agrees to go out with him again - but the appearance of his former girlfriend Faye, who has been out of the country for a while, complicates matters. Comedy, guest starring Virginia Madsen and Amy Brenneman

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: A selection of Timothy West and Prunella Scales' adventures along the waterways of Wales and the West Country, which have woven through their childhoods, marriage, and family life, and remain their favourite place of escape. programme.title: The Yorkshire Jobcentre
programme.desc: The team of job coaches gives advice to a 58-year-old recently made redundant from his job in IT, where he'd worked for nearly 20 years. A single mother from the Czech Republic who has recently moved to Leeds following a break-up applies for universal credit, but has to take a residence test to determine whether she will be eligible for anything

programme.title: Swingers
programme.desc: Documentary about the scene in which people engage in group sex or swap sexual partners, going behind the scenes at one of Britain's most popular swingers' clubs to find out what kinds of people attend and why

programme.desc: This time, Take That's Mark Owen brings his 18-month-old Doberman, who needs treatment on a limp, into the surgery. Meanwhile, a four-year-old Coton-Tzu from Dublin with hip dysplasia may require a double hip replacement, and a Border Collie is brought in needing assistance with a problem with lameness

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge help Natalie and Pete come up with some compact camping ideas, such as a portable solar shower that doubles up as a sink and tyre chairs with a rustic rope finish. Dick also helps a viewer transform her garden into a plot she can be proud of, and Angel offers upholstery advice to a woman whose favourite chair has been left in tatters by the family dog

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourites
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver puts his twist on cauliflower cheese as he teams it with spaghetti to create a creamy, silky smooth pasta dish topped off with golden crunchy breadcrumbs. Reinventing another family favourite, Jamie offers a healthier take on the classic chicken Kiev, wrapping the meat in filo pastry instead of breadcrumbs to create light, crispy parcels, while the chef introduces his son Buddy to Scotch bonnet chillies in a spicy barbecue chicken burger, with a tip to manage the heat to suit any member of the family

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Delroy and Kevin face a web of deceit in an Italian delicatessen in south London as they chase a huge debt amounting to £29,000. Meanwhile, Steve and Paul must evict a tenant in north London who has not paid her rent for over 18 months, before heading to Cambridgeshire to make one of their most emotional evictions to date. Plus, Brian and Delroy track down a pensioner in Surrey who owes £15,000 for a business he bought four years ago

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The enforcement agents try to mediate a dispute in a takeaway, and deal with rent evaders living in potentially deadly conditions. Plus, a businessman faces financial ruin

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The debt collection agents grapple with emotional evictions and moral dilemmas during the festive period, including a young family in Crystal Palace who are made homeless when the landlord repossesses their home because he wants to redevelop the property. The eviction comes as a shock to the family as they are up to date with their rent, but have just one hour to gather their belongings. Plus, an acrimonious divorce in Chessington comes to a head when the estranged wife obtains a writ to reclaim the house, and an off-licence owner in Hampshire with unpaid rent of more than £5,000 battles to keep his business open just as everything shuts down for the holidays

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Elmore are met with lies and aggression when they track down nearly £3,000 owed by a garage mechanic, while Steve and Ben encounter a retired couple who are being forced to leave their home. Stuart and Vic revisit a young debtor who owe £750 for a laptop computer, and in Oxfordshire, Paul and Steve chase £30,000 owed by a world-famous Formula One racing team

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Iain head to Manchester, where a landlord is owed £8,000 in rent arrears and has already called for police back-up. As tempers rise, the pair must find a way to handle a situation that is spiralling dangerously out of control. Meanwhile, Brian and Del chase £3,000 owed to a firm of letting agents, before tracking a debtor who was accidentally over-paid for a job. Plus, Stewart and Iain face an eviction that is set to test their patience

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The tale of an elderly couple whose hopes of a peaceful retirement were ruined when their tenants stopped paying rent, then moved in their abusive friends who became squatters. With the house becoming a free-for-all and in a terrible state of repair, and the couple facing a barrage of verbal threats and abusive behaviour, they have been forced to seek help in an attempt to get their property back. Plus, a man who cannot sell his house because his sitting tenant refuses to leave

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: In Bristol, eviction lawyer Chris deals with a complex case in which squatters have broken into an empty pub and are now refusing to leave, with landlord Alex wanting them out as he is awaiting planning permission to turn the pub into flats. Jill is trying to regain a property she and her late husband bought as an investment, which she has been renting out to alleviate financial stress

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Harrow Council carries out early morning raids on several dangerous properties, only to find vulnerable tenants in uninhabitable conditions, including one man living in a plywood shed on a roof. Plus, two more cases of nightmare tenants. Nisha lost her job just as her tenants stopped paying the rent, while Andy agreed to rent his north London home to a friend - who ended up subletting the house and pocketing the profits for himself

Thriller, adapted from the novel by John Grisham, starring Tom Cruise, Gene Hackman, Jeanne Tripplehorn and Holly Hunter

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The enforcement officers visit a former professional footballer who owes £4,500 in unpaid legal fees, and in London the officers chase up a £4,000 debt owed by a car dealer. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The officers chase a debt of almost £2,000 in unpaid parking fines, and later encounter the extraordinary case of a debt of £80,000 owed by a hotel owner to his ex-girlfriend. However, as they begin to search the property, they call upon police back-up as the agents fear the debtor may have access to firearms. Elsewhere, two officers head to a car garage in Manchester where the owner owes £2,000 after losing a court case over a faulty vehicle

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents chase more than £1,700 in unpaid parking fines in south-east London, but struggle to pinpoint the debtor. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Liam and Ben attempt to catch a gang of late-night burglars, but one of the suspects treats an officer to a face full of saliva, while Jacko and Simon are in pursuit of a car round the streets of Ferryhill, but it's not the interceptors who make the arrest. programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.desc: The hidden army of enforcement teams and inspectors out to nail the litter louts and the fearless sewage warriors clearing the mess. In Wolverhampton, cleaners Jakob and Wojtek face a toxic clean-up thanks to someone storing 50 bottles of urine in a cupboard, while across the city a crime scene is in urgent need of a deep clean

programme.desc: Jessica unwittingly gets involved in a contract killing, leading her to be accused of being the assassin and jailed. The incarcerated detective must rely on Grady and Michael to prove her innocence. With guest stars John Rhys-Davies and Michael Horton

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler investigate the rape of a cellist, and initially believe her conductor is to blame for the attack - especially given his penchant for erotic film-making. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Cabot takes a child abuse case, determined to find evidence that will convict the defendant. Her personal feelings tempt her to bend the rules, leading Stabler and Benson into an illegal search of the victim's home - a move that threatens all of their careers

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A urologist is murdered, and his wife claims she was raped and robbed by the killer. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and another officer team up to track down a serial killer, focusing their investigation on a recently released sex offender - but fresh evidence leads them to believe he may have been wrongly convicted. Concerned that a miscarriage of justice could have occurred, they look over old case files and turn their attention to the suspect's neighbour, a man with employment links in the vicinity of seven murders around the country. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and David Keith

programme.title: Rookie Blue
programme.desc: Andy and Nick continue to work undercover infiltrating a meth pipeline, but their covert mission goes sideways when both disappear. Meanwhile, a random arrest at 15 Division puts Sam Swarek and the other officers in the middle of the drug investigation-turned-rescue operation. Andy must figure out how to cope with the transformation of her own life as well as those of her friends at the station and continue to do the job she loves. Starring Missy Peregrym

programme.title: Rookie Blue

She then puts her knowledge of the criminal mind to good use by covering her tracks, and although Columbo strongly suspects foul play, he finds clues are thin on the ground. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk and Ruth Gordon

programme.title: Columbo: The Most Dangerous Match (1973)
programme.desc: An American chess champion fears he is about to be beaten by a superior player from Russia, and decides the only sure-fire way he can avoid defeat is by murdering his opponent. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Laurence Harvey and Jack Kruschen

programme.title: Columbo: It's All in the Game (1993)
programme.desc: A wealthy socialite and her daughter discover they have been seeing the same man, and have him killed. When Columbo investigates the case, the mother is determined to make sure all his attention is on her and not her daughter, and an unlikely romance develops between sleuth and suspect. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk and Faye Dunaway

programme.title: Columbo: A Case of Immunity (1975)
programme.desc: A senior adviser to the king of a Middle Eastern nation plots to increase his own power by having the head of security at their American embassy killed and placing the blame on a group of dissidents. Columbo is assigned to crack the case, but the suspect's diplomatic immunity hinders the progress of the investigation. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Hector Elizondo and Sal Mineo

programme.title: Columbo: Double Shock (1973)
programme.desc: A wealthy man due to marry a younger woman is electrocuted in the bath. Columbo suspects the victim's identical twin nephews - a flamboyant TV chef and a conservative banker - who stand to inherit his fortune if he died unmarried, but the detective is unsure which of them is the guilty party. Drama, starring Peter Falk, and Martin Landau in a dual role. With Paul Stewart, Julie Newmar and Jeanette Nolan

programme.title: Columbo: Death Lends a Hand (1971)
programme.desc: The crumpled cop investigates when a private eye murders his client's adulterous wife because she refuses to submit to his blackmail threats, and arranges the crime scene to make it look as if she was the victim of a mugging gone wrong. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Ray Milland and Robert Culp

programme.title: Columbo: Caution - Murder Can Be Hazardous to Your Health (1991)
programme.desc: The host of a TV show is threatened by a rival, who has discovered evidence of his murky past. The presenter does away with his blackmailer using poisoned cigarettes, and seems to have pulled off the perfect crime - but a certain shabby sleuth is soon on the case and finds a few things that fail to add up. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk and George Hamilton

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: The detectives investigate when it is revealed that a series of female murder victims are all ex-girlfriends of a notoriously abusive lawyer. Starring Vincent D'Onofrio, Kathryn Erbe and Jamey Sheridan

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The stepdaughter of a judge known for his harsh sentencing of sex offenders is raped and murdered. Stabler and Benson's investigations reveal the rebellious girl had been involved with defendants in her stepfather's cases - and a revelation from her childhood leads the detectives to point the finger of suspicion at the victim's nearest and dearest. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler investigate an assault on a restaurateur's wife, and find the attack is similar to a rape committed the previous week. They discover a locksmith visited both women's houses before the incident, but when they examine the stolen goods found in his toolbox, a new connection emerges - putting another suspect in the frame. Crime drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When a man is shot outside a gay club, the detectives' investigations lead to his family, where they discover the wife of the dead man has a turbulent past. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.desc: A wealthy Wall Street stockbroker's death looks like suicide, but Logan suspects otherwise and pins the blame on the man's protege. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.desc: Tensions are high between new partners Briscoe and Curtis as they investigate the murder of a wealthy student in a biker bar. The detectives discover the victim's father had offered him $10,000 to drop his hobby and his biker girlfriend. Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt star

Starring Michael Weatherly and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: As Gibbs recovers from his gunshot wound, the team is called in to investigate the killing of a US Marine and tries to determine whether the crime is related to an alleged case of blackmail at an illegal nightclub. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS

programme.desc: Detectives Benson and Stabler check the handbag of a suspected rape victim for identification and discover the severed finger of a small child in a plastic bag. programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong

programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Age of Stupid (2008)
programme.desc: Drama documentary examining the impact of climate change. In an environmentally devastated future, the last man alive looks back at footage from 2008 to uncover the reasons why the human race did so little to prevent climate change before it was too late, examining real life stories including a windfarm developer fighting public opposition and an elderly mountain guide who has witnessed the melting of Alpine glaciers first-hand

programme.title: Dead Ringers (1988)
programme.desc: Identical twin gynaecologists Elliot and Beverly share everything in their life. Elliot, self-assured and experienced, passes his unwitting sexual conquests to shy and sensitive Beverly when he tires of them. However, when Beverly falls in love for the first time with a patient, the affair drives the brothers apart - and both men begin to lose their grip on their sanity. David Cronenberg's psychological thriller, with Jeremy Irons in a dual role alongside Genevieve Bujold

programme.title: Burning an Illusion (1981)
programme.desc: A black woman living in West London hopes to marry her boyfriend, but finds her life thrown into upheaval when he loses his job and is subsequently arrested after getting into a fight. The experience makes her reassess her priorities, and she decides to reconnect with her African heritage. Drama, starring Cassie McFarlane and Victor Romero Evans

The youngsters rally the local community to save their beach, but the discovery of disused mines complicates things further. Family drama, starring Richard Adam and Fiona Davie

programme.desc: A Victorian orphan is sent to live with her uncle, and is troubled by a ghostly presence that haunts his house. Supernatural mystery, starring Sarah Hollis Andrews and Ronald Adam
